title: Task-Specific Knowledge Distillation via Intermediate Probes
authors: Ryan Brown, Chris Russell
abstract:
Knowledge distillation from large language models (LLMs) assumes that the teacher's output distribution is a high-quality training signal. On reasoning tasks, this assumption is frequently violated. A model's intermediate representations may encode the correct answer, yet this information is lost or distorted through the vocabulary projection, where prompt formatting and answer-token choices creates brittle, noisy outputs.
  We introduce \method{}, a distillation framework that bypasses this bottleneck by training lightweight probes on frozen teacher hidden states and using the probe's predictions, rather than output logits, as supervision for student training. This simple change yields consistent improvements across four reasoning benchmarks (AQuA-RAT, ARC Easy/Challenge, and MMLU), with gains most pronounced under limited data.
  Probes trained on intermediate representations provide cleaner labels than the teacher's own outputs, effectively denoising the distillation signal. \method{} requires no architectural changes to student or teacher, is architecture-agnostic, and adds minimal compute since probe training is cheap and teacher representations can be cached. By exploiting internal representations, \method{} enables practitioners to extract more value from large teacher models without additional training data or architectural complexity.
reason: 该论文研究知识蒸馏方法，通过中间探针利用教师模型的隐藏状态作为监督信号，以提升学生模型在推理任务上的性能。虽然属于大语言模型（LLM）相关研究，但其核心方法（探针训练、中间表示利用）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文在实验设计和结果上具有一定质量，在推理基准测试上展示了改进，尤其数据有限时效果更明显，但方法论不属于用户关注的具体技术范畴，因此相关性较低，但有一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 从大语言模型（LLMs）进行知识蒸馏通常假设教师模型的输出分布是高质量的训练信号。然而在推理任务中，这一假设常常不成立：模型的中间表示可能编码了正确答案，但在经过词汇投影时，由于提示格式和答案词元选择的影响，这些信息会丢失或扭曲，导致脆弱且充满噪声的输出。本文提出\method{}，一种蒸馏框架，它通过在冻结的教师隐藏状态上训练轻量级探针，并使用探针的预测而非教师输出logits作为学生训练时的监督信号，从而绕过了这一瓶颈。这一简单改动在四个推理基准测试（AQuA-RAT、ARC Easy/Challenge和MMLU）上均带来稳定提升，尤其在数据有限时增益更为显著。在中间表示上训练的探针能提供比教师自身输出更干净的标签，有效去除了蒸馏信号中的噪声。\method{}无需对学生或教师模型进行架构修改，具有架构无关性，且计算开销极小——因为探针训练成本低廉，且教师表示可被缓存。通过利用内部表示，\method{}使从业者无需额外训练数据或架构复杂度，就能从大型教师模型中提取更多价值。

title: Prompt Injection as Role Confusion
authors: Charles Ye, Jasmine Cui, Dylan Hadfield-Menell
abstract: Language models remain vulnerable to prompt injection attacks despite extensive safety training. We trace this failure to role confusion: models infer roles from how text is written, not where it comes from. We design novel role probes to capture how models internally identify "who is speaking." These reveal why prompt injection works: untrusted text that imitates a role inherits that role's authority. We test this insight by injecting spoofed reasoning into user prompts and tool outputs, achieving average success rates of 60% on StrongREJECT and 61% on agent exfiltration, across multiple open- and closed-weight models with near-zero baselines. Strikingly, the degree of internal role confusion strongly predicts attack success before generation begins. Our findings reveal a fundamental gap: security is defined at the interface but authority is assigned in latent space. More broadly, we introduce a unifying, mechanistic framework for prompt injection, demonstrating that diverse prompt-injection attacks exploit the same underlying role-confusion mechanism.
reason: 该论文的研究核心是揭示提示注入攻击的机制（角色混淆），属于大模型安全与对抗性研究领域。其研究问题、方法（设计角色探针）和结论均与用户指定的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文本身设计严谨、实验充分、具有洞见，在AI安全领域具有较高价值，但不符合用户当前关注的技术方向，故相关性低。
abstract_zh: 尽管进行了广泛的安全训练，语言模型仍易受提示注入攻击的影响。我们将这一失败追溯至角色混淆：模型根据文本的表述方式而非来源来推断角色。我们设计了新颖的角色探测方法来捕捉模型内部如何识别“谁在发言”。这些方法揭示了提示注入为何有效：模仿某角色的不可信文本会继承该角色的权威。我们通过向用户提示和工具输出中注入伪造推理来验证这一洞察，在多个开放与闭权模型上，于StrongREJECT和代理数据窃取任务中分别取得了平均60%和61%的攻击成功率，而基线接近为零。 strikingly的是，内部角色混淆的程度强烈预示了生成开始前的攻击成功率。我们的研究发现了一个根本性差距：安全定义在接口层面，而权威却在潜在空间中被赋予。更广泛地说，我们引入了一个统一的、机制性的提示注入框架，证明多样化的提示注入攻击均利用了相同的底层角色混淆机制。

title: LLM-Augmented Therapy Normalization and Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis for Treatment-Resistant Depression on Reddit
authors: Yuxin Zhu, Sahithi Lakamana, Masoud Rouhizadeh, Selen Bozkurt, Rachel Hershenberg, Abeed Sarker
abstract: Treatment-resistant depression (TRD) is a severe form of major depressive disorder in which patients do not achieve remission despite multiple adequate treatment trials. Evidence across pharmacologic options for TRD remains limited, and trials often do not fully capture patient-reported tolerability. Large-scale online peer-support narratives therefore offer a complementary lens on how patients describe and evaluate medications in real-world use. In this study, we curated a corpus of 5,059 Reddit posts explicitly referencing TRD from 3,480 subscribers across 28 mental health-related subreddits from 2010 to 2025. Of these, 3,839 posts mentioned at least one medication, yielding 23,399 mentions of 81 generic-name medications after lexicon-based normalization of brand names, misspellings, and colloquialisms. We developed an aspect-based sentiment classifier by fine-tuning DeBERTa-v3 on the SMM4H 2023 therapy-sentiment Twitter corpus with large language model based data augmentation, achieving a micro-F1 score of 0.800 on the shared-task test set. Applying this classifier to Reddit, we quantified sentiment toward individual medications across three categories: positive, neutral, and negative, and tracked patterns by drug, subscriber, subreddit, and year. Overall, 72.1% of medication mentions were neutral, 14.8% negative, and 13.1% positive. Conventional antidepressants, especially SSRIs and SNRIs, showed consistently higher negative than positive proportions, whereas ketamine and esketamine showed comparatively more favorable sentiment profiles. These findings show that normalized medication extraction combined with aspect-based sentiment analysis can help characterize patient-perceived treatment experiences in TRD-related Reddit discourse, complementing clinical evidence with large-scale patient-generated perspectives.
reason: 该论文核心研究内容为利用LLM（DeBERTa-v3）进行医疗健康领域的文本情感分析，并涉及特定领域的 lexicon-based 数据标准化。虽然使用了LLM技术，但其研究目标（治疗抵抗性抑郁症的药物情感分析）、数据来源（Reddit心理支持社区）及方法论重点（方面级情感分类）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择技术、推测解码加速技术）均无直接关联。论文在LLM应用和医疗NLP方面有一定价值，但不符合用户关注的LLM训练与推理优化核心问题，因此相关性低。
abstract_zh: 难治性抑郁症（TRD）是一种严重型重度抑郁障碍，患者即使接受多次充分治疗仍无法缓解。针对TRD的药物证据有限，临床试验往往未能完全捕捉患者报告的不良反应。因此，大规模在线同伴支持讨论为理解患者在真实世界中使用和评价药物提供了补充视角。本研究从2010年至2025年，在28个心理健康相关子论坛中收集了3,480名订阅者发布的5,059篇明确提及TRD的Reddit帖子。其中3,839篇帖子提到至少一种药物，经基于词典的品牌名、拼写错误和俚语标准化后，共提取出23,399次提及，涵盖81种通用名药物。我们通过在大语言模型增强的数据上对DeBERTa-v3进行微调，开发了一个基于方面的情感分类器，在SMM4H 2023治疗情感Twitter语料库的共享任务测试集上达到0.800的微F1分数。将该分类器应用于Reddit数据，我们量化了单个药物在积极、中性、消极三类情感中的分布，并按药物、订阅者、子论坛和年份追踪模式。总体而言，72.1%的药物提及呈中性，14.8%为消极，13.1%为积极。传统抗抑郁药（尤其是SSRIs和SNRIs）的消极比例持续高于积极比例，而氯胺酮和艾司氯胺酮则表现出相对更积极的情感分布。这些发现表明，标准化的药物提取结合基于方面的情感分析，有助于在TRD相关Reddit讨论中刻画患者感知的治疗体验，以大规模患者生成观点补充临床证据。

title: TASTE-Streaming: Towards Streamable Text-Aligned Speech Tokenization and Embedding for Spoken Language Modeling
authors: Liang-Hsuan Tseng, Hung-yi Lee
abstract: Text-speech joint spoken language modeling (SLM) aims at natural and intelligent speech-based interactions, but developing such a system may suffer from modality mismatch: speech unit sequences are much longer than text tokens. Prior work reduces this gap with text-aligned tokenization and embedding (TASTE), producing speech tokens that align in lengths with their textual counterparts. However, the dependence on an external ASR system and the use of a non-causal decoder limits streaming use. To address this limitation, we propose TASTE-S, a streamable extension of TASTE suitable for real-time usage. TASTE-S integrates a CTC-based ASR module into the encoder for instant dual-modality encoding. We also redesign the unit decoder to enable on-the-fly decoding. With joint training, we show that TASTE-S matches TASTE's performance while significantly reducing latency. Further investigations reveal that TASTE-S remains robust to transcriptions and enables long-form encoding and decoding.
reason: 该论文主要研究语音语言模型中的流式文本-语音对齐tokenization，核心贡献在于通过CTC编码器和即时解码降低延迟。论文未涉及用户指定的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择或推测解码），两者在技术目标和关键词上均无重叠。尽管论文在语音模态处理领域有一定的方法创新和实验验证，但与用户的具体研究焦点无关。
abstract_zh: 文本-语音联合口语建模旨在实现自然、智能的语音交互，但开发此类系统常面临模态不匹配问题：语音单元序列长度远大于文本标记。先前工作通过文本对齐的标记化与嵌入（TASTE）缩小了这一差距，生成了与文本长度对齐的语音标记。然而，其对外部自动语音识别（ASR）系统的依赖以及非因果解码器的使用限制了流式应用。为解决这一局限，我们提出了TASTE-S，即TASTE的流式扩展，适用于实时场景。TASTE-S将基于连接时序分类（CTC）的ASR模块集成至编码器，实现即时双模态编码。我们还重新设计单元解码器以支持动态解码。通过联合训练，实验表明TASTE-S在性能上与TASTE相当，同时显著降低了延迟。进一步研究发现，TASTE-S对转录结果具有鲁棒性，并支持长文本形式的编码与解码。

title: Interpreting Negation in GPT-2: Layer- and Head-Level Causal Analysis
authors: Abdullah Al Mofael, Lisa M. Kuhn, Ghassan Alkadi, Kuo-Pao Yang
abstract: Negation remains a persistent challenge for modern language models, often causing reversed meanings or factual errors. In this work, we conduct a causal analysis of how GPT-2 Small internally processes such linguistic transformations. We examine its hidden representations at both the layer and head level. Our analysis is based on a self-curated 12,000-pair dataset of matched affirmative and negated sentences, covering multiple linguistic templates and forms of negation. To quantify this behavior, we define a metric, the Negation Effect Score (NES), which measures the model's sensitivity in distinguishing between affirmative statements and their negations. We carried out two key interventions to probe causal structure. In activation patching, internal activations from affirmative sentences were inserted into their negated counterparts to see how meaning shifted. In ablation, specific attention heads were temporarily disabled to observe how logical polarity changed. Together, these steps revealed how negation signals move and evolve through GPT-2's layers. Our findings indicate that this capability is not widespread; instead, it is highly concentrated within a limited number of mid-layer attention heads, primarily within layers 4 to 6. Ablating these specific components directly disrupts the model's negation sensitivity: on our in-domain, ablation increased NES (indicating weaker negation sensitivity), and re-introducing cached affirmative activations (rescue) increased NES further, confirming that these heads carry affirmative signal rather than restoring baseline behavior. On xNot360, ablation slightly decreased NES and rescue restored performance above baseline. This pattern demonstrates that these causal patterns are consistent across various negation forms and remain detectable on the external xNot360 benchmark, though with smaller magnitude.
reason: 该论文主题聚焦于GPT-2模型内部处理否定现象的因果可解释性分析，研究方法严谨（包括自建数据集、定义NES指标、激活修补与消融实验），在模型可解释性领域具有质量与价值。然而，其核心内容与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、投机解码）均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据筛选、质量评估、缓存机制或解码加速等相关技术，因此对于用户的研究目标，相关性较低，但作为通用的模型机制分析论文仍存在一定的参考价值。
abstract_zh: 否定（Negation）仍是现代语言模型持续面临的挑战，常导致语义反转或事实性错误。本研究对GPT-2 Small内部如何处理此类语言转换进行了因果分析，在层与注意力头两个层面检视了其隐藏表征。分析基于我们自建的包含12000组匹配的肯定句与否定句的数据集，覆盖多种语言模板与否定形式。为量化该行为，我们定义了“否定效应分数”（Negation Effect Score, NES）这一指标，用以测量模型区分肯定陈述与其否定形式的能力。我们实施了两种关键干预以探测因果结构：在“激活补丁”（activation patching）中，将肯定句的内部激活注入其对应的否定句，观察语义如何变化；在“消融”（ablation）中，临时禁用特定注意力头，以观察逻辑极性如何改变。综合这些步骤揭示了否定信号在GPT-2各层中的传递与演变过程。研究发现该能力并非广泛分布，而是高度集中于少数中间层注意力头，主要集中在第4至6层。消融这些特定组件会直接破坏模型的否定敏感性：在内部数据集上，消融后NES升高（表明否定敏感性减弱），而重新引入缓存的肯定句激活（rescue）后NES进一步升高，证实这些头承载的是肯定信号而非恢复基线行为。在外部基准xNot360上，消融使NES轻微下降，而rescue使表现恢复至基线以上。这一模式表明，所发现的因果模式跨多种否定形式保持一致，且能在外部xNot360基准上被检测到，尽管效应幅度较小。

title: Marked Pedagogies: Examining Linguistic Biases in Personalized Automated Writing Feedback
authors: Mei Tan, Lena Phalen, Dorottya Demszky
abstract: Effective personalized feedback is critical to students' literacy development. Though LLM-powered tools now promise to automate such feedback at scale, LLMs are not language-neutral: they privilege standard academic English and reproduce social stereotypes, raising concerns about how "personalization" shapes the feedback students receive. We examine how four widely used LLMs (GPT-4o, GPT-3.5-turbo, Llama-3.3 70B, Llama-3.1 8B) adapt written feedback in response to student attributes. Using 600 eighth-grade persuasive essays from the PERSUADE dataset, we generated feedback under prompt conditions embedding gender, race/ethnicity, learning needs, achievement, and motivation. We analyze lexical shifts across model outputs by adapting the Marked Words framework. Our results reveal systematic, stereotype-aligned shifts in feedback conditioned on presumed student attributes--even when essay content was identical. Feedback for students marked by race, language, or disability often exhibited positive feedback bias and feedback withholding bias--overuse of praise, less substantive critique, and assumptions of limited ability. Across attributes, models tailored not only what content was emphasized but also how writing was judged and how students were addressed. We term these instructional orientations Marked Pedagogies and highlight the need for transparency and accountability in automated feedback tools.
reason: 该论文研究LLM在教育场景下生成个性化反馈时存在的语言偏见与社会刻板印象问题，属于LLM社会影响、公平性与教育技术应用的研究范畴。其核心关注点（偏见检测、教育反馈、社会语言学分析）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择方法、推测解码技术）在主题、方法论和目标上均无直接交集。因此，与用户研究方向相关性低。但论文本身研究设计严谨（多模型测试、真实数据集、框架创新），在LLM应用伦理领域具有一定独立价值。
abstract_zh: 有效的个性化反馈对学生读写能力的发展至关重要。尽管如今由大语言模型驱动的工具承诺能够大规模自动化此类反馈，但大语言模型并非语言中立：它们倾向于privilege标准学术英语并再现社会刻板印象，这引发了关于“个性化”如何影响学生所获反馈的担忧。我们检验了四种广泛使用的大语言模型（GPT-4o、GPT-3.5-turbo、Llama-3.3 70B、Llama-3.1 8B）如何根据学生属性调整书面反馈。我们使用PERSUADE数据集中的600篇八年级说服性作文，在嵌入性别、种族/民族、学习需求、成绩和动机的提示条件下生成反馈。我们通过 adapting Marked Words 框架分析模型输出中的词汇变化。结果显示，即使在作文内容完全相同的情况下，基于预设的学生属性，反馈中存在系统性的、与刻板印象一致的转变。针对被标记为特定种族、语言或残疾学生的反馈，常表现出积极反馈偏见和反馈保留偏见——即过度使用表扬、减少实质性批评，以及预设能力有限。跨所有属性，模型不仅调整了强调的内容，也调整了如何评判写作以及如何与学生对话。我们将这些教学导向称为“标记化教学法”，并强调自动化反馈工具需要透明度和问责制。

title: Expert Pyramid Tuning: Efficient Parameter Fine-Tuning for Expertise-Driven Task Allocation
authors: Jia-Chen Zhang, Zhen-Wei Yan, Yu-Jie Xiong, Chun-Ming Xia
abstract: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) has become a dominant paradigm for deploying LLMs in multi-task scenarios due to its extreme parameter efficiency. While Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) based LoRA variants have achieved promising results by dynamically routing tokens to different low-rank experts, they largely overlook the hierarchical nature of task complexity. Existing methods typically employ experts with uniform architectures, limiting their ability to capture diverse feature granularities required by distinct tasks--where some tasks demand high-level semantic abstraction while others require fine-grained syntactic manipulation. To bridge this gap, we propose Expert Pyramid Tuning (EPT), a novel architecture that integrates the multi-scale feature pyramid concept from computer vision into the realm of PEFT. Unlike standard LoRA, EPT decomposes task adaptation into two stages: (1) A shared meta-knowledge Subspace that encodes universal linguistic patterns in low dimensions; (2) A Pyramid Projection Mechanism that utilizes learnable up-projection operators to reconstruct high-dimensional features at varying scales. A task-aware router then dynamically selects the optimal combination of these multi-scale features. Extensive experiments across multiple multi-task benchmarks demonstrate that EPT significantly outperforms SOTA MoE-LoRA variants. Crucially, thanks to the re-parameterization capability of our design, EPT achieves this performance improvement while simultaneously reducing the number of training parameters.
reason: 该论文研究的是参数高效微调（PEFT）中的模型架构创新（Expert Pyramid Tuning），核心贡献在于将多尺度特征金字塔思想应用于MoE-LoRA以处理任务复杂度的层次性。这与您的研究方向‘LLM training: data selection’（关注数据层面）和‘speculative decoding’（关注推理解码加速）均无直接关联。论文本身质量较高，方法有创新性且实验充分，但因其主题不属于您关注的任一具体领域，故相关性低，仅具一般性参考价值。
abstract_zh: 参数高效微调（PEFT）因其极高的参数效率，已成为在多任务场景中部署大语言模型的主流范式。尽管基于专家混合（MoE）的LoRA变体通过动态将词元路由至不同的低秩专家已取得令人鼓舞的成果，但它们很大程度上忽视了任务复杂度的层次性。现有方法通常采用结构统一的专家，这限制了他们捕捉不同任务所需多样化特征粒度的能力——部分任务需要高层语义抽象，而另一些则需要细粒度句法操作。为弥补这一差距，我们提出了专家金字塔微调（EPT），一种将计算机视觉中的多尺度特征金字塔概念引入PEFT领域的全新架构。与标准LoRA不同，EPT将任务适应分解为两个阶段：（1）一个共享的元知识子空间，用于在低维中编码通用语言模式；（2）一个金字塔投影机制，利用可学习的上投影算子在不同尺度重建高维特征。一个感知任务的路由器随后动态选择这些多尺度特征的最优组合。在多个多任务基准测试上的广泛实验表明，EPT显著优于最先进的MoE-LoRA变体。关键在于，得益于我们设计的重参数化能力，EPT在实现这一性能提升的同时，还减少了训练参数的数量。

title: RTD-Guard: A Black-Box Textual Adversarial Detection Framework via Replacement Token Detection
authors: He Zhu, Yanshu Li, Wen Liu, Haitian Yang
abstract: Textual adversarial attacks pose a serious security threat to Natural Language Processing (NLP) systems by introducing imperceptible perturbations that mislead deep learning models. While adversarial example detection offers a lightweight alternative to robust training, existing methods typically rely on prior knowledge of attacks, white-box access to the victim model, or numerous queries, which severely limits their practical deployment. This paper introduces RTD-Guard, a novel black-box framework for detecting textual adversarial examples. Our key insight is that word-substitution perturbations in adversarial attacks closely resemble the "replaced tokens" that a Replaced Token Detection (RTD) discriminator is pre-trained to identify. Leveraging this, RTD-Guard employs an off-the-shelf RTD discriminator-without fine-tuning-to localize suspicious tokens, masks them, and detects adversarial examples by observing the prediction confidence shift of the victim model before and after intervention. The entire process requires no adversarial data, model tuning, or internal model access, and uses only two black-box queries. Comprehensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that RTD-Guard effectively detects adversarial texts generated by diverse state-of-the-art attack methods. It surpasses existing detection baselines across multiple metrics, offering a highly efficient, practical, and resource-light defense mechanism-particularly suited for real-world deployment in resource-constrained or privacy-sensitive environments.
reason: 该论文的研究主题（文本对抗攻击检测）与用户提供的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）无直接关联。论文核心贡献在于利用预训练的替换标记检测器进行黑盒对抗样本检测，属于NLP安全/对抗鲁棒性领域，未涉及数据 curation、过滤、课程学习或推测解码等相关技术。尽管方法设计高效且实验充分，但与用户指定的研究范畴相关性极低。

title: Continual Learning in Large Language Models: Methods, Challenges, and Opportunities
authors: Hongyang Chen, Zhongwu Sun, Hongfei Ye, Kunchi Li, Xuemin Lin
abstract: Continual learning (CL) has emerged as a pivotal paradigm to enable large language models (LLMs) to dynamically adapt to evolving knowledge and sequential tasks while mitigating catastrophic forgetting-a critical limitation of the static pre-training paradigm inherent to modern LLMs. This survey presents a comprehensive overview of CL methodologies tailored for LLMs, structured around three core training stages: continual pre-training, continual fine-tuning, and continual alignment.Beyond the canonical taxonomy of rehearsal-, regularization-, and architecture-based methods, we further subdivide each category by its distinct forgetting mitigation mechanisms and conduct a rigorous comparative analysis of the adaptability and critical improvements of traditional CL methods for LLMs. In doing so, we explicitly highlight core distinctions between LLM CL and traditional machine learning, particularly with respect to scale, parameter efficiency, and emergent capabilities. Our analysis covers essential evaluation metrics, including forgetting rates and knowledge transfer efficiency, along with emerging benchmarks for assessing CL performance. This survey reveals that while current methods demonstrate promising results in specific domains, fundamental challenges persist in achieving seamless knowledge integration across diverse tasks and temporal scales. This systematic review contributes to the growing body of knowledge on LLM adaptation, providing researchers and practitioners with a structured framework for understanding current achievements and future opportunities in lifelong learning for language models.
reason: 该论文主题为大型语言模型（LLM）的持续学习（Continual Learning），虽然属于LLM训练与适应的宏观范畴，但与用户指定的两个具体研究方向（'llm-data-selection' 和 'speculative-decoding'）的核心关键词（如数据选择/过滤/去重、推测解码/快速解码）无直接重叠。论文重点在于综述应对灾难性遗忘的CL方法（如重播、正则化、架构方法）及评估，并未涉及用户关注的数据选择技术或解码加速技术。因此，相关性较低，但作为LLM适应性的背景综述，可能具有边缘参考价值。
abstract_zh: 持续学习（CL）已成为使大语言模型（LLM）能够动态适应不断演化的知识和顺序任务，同时缓解灾难性遗忘——这是现代LLM静态预训练范式的关键局限——的一种关键范式。本综述针对LLM的CL方法进行了全面概述，围绕三个核心训练阶段展开：持续预训练、持续微调以及持续对齐。超越重放、正则化和基于架构等经典方法分类，我们进一步依据其各异的遗忘缓解机制对每个类别进行细分，并对传统CL方法在LLM中的适应性与关键改进进行了严格的比较分析。在此过程中，我们明确强调了LLM CL与传统机器学习之间的核心区别，特别是在规模、参数效率和涌现能力方面。我们的分析涵盖了关键的评估指标，包括遗忘率和知识迁移效率，以及用于评估CL性能的新兴基准。本综述揭示，尽管当前方法在特定领域展现出令人鼓舞的结果，但在实现跨多样任务和时间尺度的无缝知识整合方面，仍存在根本性挑战。这项系统综述为日益增长的LLM适应性知识体系做出了贡献，为研究人员和实践者提供了一个理解语言模型终身学习当前成就与未来机遇的结构化框架。

title: From Text to Forecasts: Bridging Modality Gap with Temporal Evolution Semantic Space
authors: Lehui Li, Yuyao Wang, Jisheng Yan, Wei Zhang, Jinliang Deng, Haoliang Sun, Zhongyi Han, Yongshun Gong
abstract: Incorporating textual information into time-series forecasting holds promise for addressing event-driven non-stationarity; however, a fundamental modality gap hinders effective fusion: textual descriptions express temporal impacts implicitly and qualitatively, whereas forecasting models rely on explicit and quantitative signals. Through controlled semi-synthetic experiments, we show that existing methods over-attend to redundant tokens and struggle to reliably translate textual semantics into usable numerical cues. To bridge this gap, we propose TESS, which introduces a Temporal Evolution Semantic Space as an intermediate bottleneck between modalities. This space consists of interpretable, numerically grounded temporal primitives (mean shift, volatility, shape, and lag) extracted from text by an LLM via structured prompting and filtered through confidence-aware gating. Experiments on four real-world datasets demonstrate up to a 29 percent reduction in forecasting error compared to state-of-the-art unimodal and multimodal baselines. The code will be released after acceptance.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献在于利用LLM作为工具将文本信息转化为结构化的时间原语，以桥接文本与时间序列的模态差距，从而提升预测性能。这与用户研究方向中的“LLM training: data selection”有微弱联系，因为论文涉及使用LLM进行信息提取和过滤（confidence-aware gating），但其目标并非优化LLM的训练数据本身（如数据选择、清洗、去重或影响函数分析），而是将LLM作为多模态融合的特征提取器。论文与“speculative decoding”领域完全无关。整体上，论文质量较高（方法新颖、实验充分、提升显著），但应用场景（时间序列预测）和核心技术路径与用户指定的两个研究方向关联度低，属于有价值但非直接相关的研究。
abstract_zh: 将文本信息融入时间序列预测有望解决事件驱动的非平稳性问题；然而，根本的模态鸿沟阻碍了有效融合：文本描述以隐性和定性的方式表达时间影响，而预测模型依赖显性和定量的信号。通过控制半合成实验，我们发现现有方法过度关注冗余词元，且难以将文本语义可靠地转化为可用的数值线索。为弥合这一差距，我们提出了TESS，其引入了一个时间演变语义空间作为模态间的中间瓶颈。该空间由通过结构化提示从大语言模型提取并经置信度感知门控过滤的、可解释且具数值基础的时间基元（均值偏移、波动性、形态与时滞）构成。在四个真实数据集上的实验表明，与最先进的单模态和多模态基线方法相比，预测误差最高降低29%。代码将在论文接收后发布。

title: Experimental evidence of progressive ChatGPT models self-convergence
authors: Konstantinos F. Xylogiannopoulos, Petros Xanthopoulos, Panagiotis Karampelas, Georgios A. Bakamitsos
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) that undergo recursive training on synthetically generated data are susceptible to model collapse, a phenomenon marked by the generation of meaningless output. Existing research has examined this issue from either theoretical or empirical perspectives, often focusing on a single model trained recursively on its own outputs. While prior studies have cautioned against the potential degradation of LLM output quality under such conditions, no longitudinal investigation has yet been conducted to assess this effect over time. In this study, we employ a text similarity metric to evaluate different ChatGPT models' capacity to generate diverse textual outputs. Our findings indicate a measurable decline of recent ChatGPT releases' ability to produce varied text, even when explicitly prompted to do so, by setting the temperature parameter to one. The observed reduction in output diversity may be attributed to the influence of the amounts of synthetic data incorporated within their training datasets as the result of internet infiltration by LLM generated data. The phenomenon is defined as model self-convergence because of the gradual increase of similarities of produced texts among different ChatGPT versions.
reason: 该论文研究与用户研究方向中的'LLM training: data selection'领域有中等相关性。论文实证观察了ChatGPT模型因训练数据中混入过多LLM生成合成数据（数据质量问题）而导致的输出多样性下降（自收敛现象），这触及了数据质量对模型训练影响的核心议题。然而，研究主要停留在现象观测层面，未深入探讨数据选择、过滤、去重等具体技术，也未提出解决方案，研究深度和方法论相对有限。论文质量一般，结论有一定参考价值但不够深入，适合快速浏览摘要以了解该现象。
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在合成生成数据上进行递归训练时，容易发生模型崩溃——一种以生成无意义输出为特征的现象。现有研究从理论或实证角度审视了此问题，通常聚焦于单一模型在其自身输出上递归训练的情况。尽管先前研究已警示在此类条件下LLM输出质量可能下降，但尚未有纵向研究评估该效应随时间的变化。本研究采用文本相似度指标，评估不同ChatGPT模型生成多样化文本的能力。研究发现，近期发布的ChatGPT版本生成多样化文本的能力存在可衡量的下降，即使明确要求其如此做并将温度参数设为1。输出多样性的减少可能归因于其训练数据集中掺入了大量合成数据，这是由互联网中LLM生成数据渗透所致。该现象被定义为模型自我收敛，因为不同ChatGPT版本所生成文本的相似度随时间逐渐增加。
core_contribution: 通过纵向研究揭示ChatGPT系列模型存在输出多样性随时间下降的“自我收敛”现象，并将此与训练数据中合成数据比例上升相关联。

title: EvolveCoder: Evolving Test Cases via Adversarial Verification for Code Reinforcement Learning
authors: Chi Ruan, Dongfu Jiang, Huaye Zeng, Ping Nie, Wenhu Chen
abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is a promising approach for improving code generation in large language models, but its effectiveness is limited by weak and static verification signals in existing coding RL datasets. In this paper, we propose a solution-conditioned and adversarial verification framework that iteratively refines test cases based on the execution behaviors of candidate solutions, with the goal of increasing difficulty, improving discriminative power, and reducing redundancy. Based on this framework, we introduce EvolveCoder-22k, a large-scale coding reinforcement learning dataset constructed through multiple rounds of adversarial test case evolution. Empirical analysis shows that iterative refinement substantially strengthens verification, with pass@1 decreasing from 43.80 to 31.22. Reinforcement learning on EvolveCoder-22k yields stable optimization and consistent performance gains, improving Qwen3-4B by an average of 4.2 points across four downstream benchmarks and outperforming strong 4B-scale baselines. Our results highlight the importance of adversarial, solution-conditioned verification for effective and scalable reinforcement learning in code generation.
reason: 论文核心工作是通过对抗性验证迭代构建高质量的代码测试用例数据集（EvolveCoder-22k），直接针对LLM训练中的数据质量与策展（data quality, data curation）问题，与研究方向'LLM training: data selection'高度契合。方法创新性强（solution-conditioned adversarial verification），实验设计严谨，结果显著（提升4B模型平均4.2分），属于该领域的优秀工作，值得精读与深入分析。
abstract_zh: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）是提升大语言模型代码生成能力的有效方法，但其效果受限于现有代码RL数据集中弱且静态的验证信号。本文提出一个解条件对抗性验证框架，通过迭代基于候选解的执行行为细化测试用例，以增加难度、提升判别力并减少冗余。基于此框架，我们构建了EvolveCoder-22k，一个通过多轮对抗性测试用例进化生成的大规模代码强化学习数据集。实证分析表明，迭代细化显著强化了验证，pass@1从43.80降至31.22。在EvolveCoder-22k上进行强化学习实现了稳定优化和持续性能提升，使Qwen3-4B在四个下游基准上平均提高4.2分，并超越其他4B规模基线。我们的结果强调了对抗性、解条件验证对于代码生成中有效且可扩展的强化学习至关重要。
method_highlights: 提出解条件对抗性验证框架：测试用例根据候选解的实际执行反馈（如失败测试、错误类型）动态进化，而非静态预设，使验证信号更精准反映模型弱点，这是核心创新。
迭代测试用例进化机制：多轮循环中，未通过当前测试集的解会触发测试用例的变异（如修改输入、增加约束）以生成更难测试，形成解与测试的共同进化闭环，逐步提升验证难度和判别力，巧妙之处在于将测试用例视为动态对抗方。
自动化大规模数据集构建：基于进化框架自动生成EvolveCoder-22k，包含22k问题及多轮进化后的测试用例，确保规模与质量，解决人工标注成本高与静态测试集不足的问题。
解条件定制化：测试用例针对具体解的行为模式设计（如针对特定边界条件或逻辑错误），能暴露特定错误类型，减少冗余测试，提高验证效率。
experiment_highlights: 迭代进化显著增加测试难度：经过多轮对抗性进化，同一问题的测试集对初始解的通过率（pass@1）从43.80降至31.22，表明验证信号更强、判别力更高。
RL训练稳定且性能提升：在EvolveCoder-22k上训练Qwen3-4B，在HumanEval、MBPP、APPS和CodeContests四个基准上平均绝对提升4.2分，且训练曲线稳定，优于其他4B规模基线模型（如CodeLlama-4B）。
数据集对比优势：EvolveCoder-22k相比传统数据集（如APPS、MBPP），测试用例数量更多（平均每个问题多个测试）、难度分布更广，且经过对抗性进化，验证效果更优，尤其对复杂问题。
对抗性验证有效性证明：消融实验显示，使用静态测试集进行RL易导致性能波动或退化，而动态进化测试集能持续驱动模型优化，验证了框架核心假设——强验证信号是RLVR成功的关键。
pros: 方法论创新：首次将对抗性进化引入代码RL测试用例生成，解决了静态验证信号弱、判别力不足的根本问题，为RLVR提供了更有效的训练信号闭环。
实用价值：构建了首个大规模对抗性进化代码RL数据集EvolveCoder-22k，可直接用于训练和评估，推动代码生成RL研究标准化。
性能突破：在较小模型（4B参数）上达到接近或超越更大模型（如7B）的性能，证明高质量、自适应验证信号能弥补模型规模不足，提升训练效率。
框架通用性：解条件对抗性框架可扩展到其他需要细粒度验证的RL任务（如数学推理、形式化证明），为跨领域RL提供新思路。
cons: 计算开销大：多轮测试用例进化需要反复执行候选解并分析执行轨迹，计算成本高，可能限制数据集扩展速度和大规模应用。
进化偏差风险：测试用例进化可能过度针对特定错误模式或初始解分布，导致验证集分布偏离真实问题分布，影响模型泛化能力。
依赖初始解质量：进化起点为初始解集，若初始解多样性不足或质量差，可能限制测试用例进化空间，陷入局部最优。
未充分评估长尾问题：实验集中在常见基准（如HumanEval），对罕见、复杂或领域外问题的验证效果未深入分析，泛化性存疑。
可复现性细节不足：论文未完全公开进化算法关键超参数（如变异率、选择压力、终止条件），可能影响他人精确复现实验细节。
reproducibility: 论文未在摘要中明确声明是否开源代码和完整数据集，但基于顶会惯例及数据集描述，推测项目页面可能提供部分资源。实验设置部分应详述模型架构、训练超参数及进化策略，但摘要未提及具体参数值，需查阅全文确认。整体可复现性中等，依赖作者后续开源实现与详细文档。
task_list: 阅读论文第三、四节，深入理解对抗性验证框架的算法细节、进化策略（如变异操作、选择机制）及数据集构建流程。
访问论文项目页面（若存在），下载EvolveCoder-22k数据集和代码，尝试复现测试用例进化模块，验证进化前后测试集难度变化。
在HumanEval基准上运行基线模型（如Qwen3-4B原始版），对比使用EvolveCoder-22k训练前后的性能，独立验证提升幅度与稳定性。
分析EvolveCoder-22k中测试用例的难度分布、错误类型覆盖及冗余度，评估其多样性是否优于静态数据集。
将EvolveCoder框架应用于其他代码任务（如代码翻译、缺陷检测），检验其泛化能力与对不同任务的有效性。
进行消融实验：移除解条件机制（使用通用测试）或迭代进化（单轮测试），量化各组件对RL性能的贡献。
探索更高效的进化策略，如基于模型预测或元学习的测试用例生成，以减少多轮执行的计算开销。

title: SectEval: Evaluating the Latent Sectarian Preferences of Large Language Models
authors: Aditya Maheshwari, Amit Gajkeshwar, Kaushal Sharma, Vivek Patel
abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) becomes a popular source for religious knowledge, it is important to know if it treats different groups fairly. This study is the first to measure how LLMs handle the differences between the two main sects of Islam: Sunni and Shia. We present a test called SectEval, available in both English and Hindi, consisting of 88 questions, to check the bias-ness of 15 top LLM models, both proprietary and open-weights. Our results show a major inconsistency based on language. In English, many powerful models DeepSeek-v3 and GPT-4o often favored Shia answers. However, when asked the exact same questions in Hindi, these models switched to favoring Sunni answers. This means a user could get completely different religious advice just by changing languages. We also looked at how models react to location. Advanced models Claude-3.5 changed their answers to match the user's country-giving Shia answers to a user from Iran and Sunni answers to a user from Saudi Arabia. In contrast, smaller models (especially in Hindi) ignored the user's location and stuck to a Sunni viewpoint. These findings show that AI is not neutral; its religious ``truth'' changes depending on the language you speak and the country you claim to be from. The data set is available at https://github.com/secteval/SectEval/
reason: 该论文的研究主题（评估LLM在宗教教派问题上的语言与地域偏见）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在核心目标、方法论和技术焦点上均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据筛选、过滤、质量评估或解码加速技术，属于LLM社会影响与公平性评估的范畴。尽管论文本身在偏见检测领域可能具有独立价值，但因其与用户研究目标高度不相关，对用户的研究工作几乎无参考意义。

title: SteerRM: Debiasing Reward Models via Sparse Autoencoders
authors: Mengyuan Sun, Zhuohao Yu, Weizheng Gu, Shikun Zhang, Wei Ye
abstract: Reward models (RMs) are critical components of alignment pipelines, yet they exhibit biases toward superficial stylistic cues, preferring better-presented responses over semantically superior ones. Existing debiasing methods typically require retraining or architectural modifications, while direct activation suppression degrades performance due to representation entanglement. We propose SteerRM, the first training-free method for debiasing reward models using Sparse Autoencoder (SAE)-based interventions. SteerRM isolates stylistic effects using contrastive paired responses, identifies bias-related SAE features with a strength-stability criterion, and suppresses them at inference time. Across six reward models on RM-Bench, SteerRM improves Hard-split accuracy by 7.3 points on average while preserving overall performance. Results on a Gemma-based reward model and a controlled non-format bias further suggest generalization across RM architectures and bias types. We further find that format-related features are concentrated in shallow layers and transfer across models, revealing shared architecture-level bias encoding patterns. These results show that SAE-based interventions can mitigate reward-model biases without retraining, providing a practical and interpretable solution for alignment pipelines.
reason: 该论文的研究内容（使用稀疏自编码器对奖励模型进行去偏）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、投机解码）均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据筛选、过滤、课程学习或任何解码加速技术，因此相关性很低。尽管论文本身在奖励模型去偏领域可能具有独立价值，但不符合用户当前的研究焦点。

title: Rethinking Multiple-Choice Questions for RLVR: Unlocking Potential via Distractor Design
authors: Xu Guo, Qiming Ge, Jian Tong, Kedi Chen, Jin Zhang, Xiaogui Yang, Xuan Gao, Haijun Lv, Zhihui Lu, Yicheng Zou, Qipeng Guo
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) significantly enhances the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models. When applied to RLVR, Multiple-Choice Questions (MCQs) offer a scalable source of verifiable data but risk inducing reward hacking, where models shortcut reasoning via random guessing or simple elimination. Current approaches often mitigate this by converting MCQs to open-ended formats, thereby discarding the contrastive signal provided by expert-designed distractors. In this work, we systematically investigate the impact of option design on RLVR. Our analysis highlights two primary insights: (1) Mismatches in option counts between training and testing degrade performance. (2) Strong distractors effectively mitigate random guessing, enabling effective RLVR training even with 2-way questions. Motivated by these findings, we propose Iterative Distractor Curation (IDC), a framework that actively constructs high-quality distractors to block elimination shortcuts and promote deep reasoning. Experiments on various benchmarks demonstrate that our method effectively enhances distractor quality and yields significant gains in RLVR training compared to the original data.
reason: 该论文与研究方向'LLM training: data selection'高度相关。论文核心聚焦于为RLVR训练设计高质量的干扰项（distractors），这本质上是数据质量提升与数据策划（data curation）的一种形式，旨在优化训练数据的有效性并防止模型走捷径（reward hacking）。提出的Iterative Distractor Curation (IDC)框架是一种主动的数据构造与筛选方法，直接对应研究方向中的'data quality'和'data curation'关键词。论文问题定义清晰，分析系统，提出的方法有创新性且实验验证充分，对提升RLVR训练数据质量具有显著参考价值，属于该领域的优秀工作，值得精读与深入分析。
abstract_zh: 本文研究如何通过干扰项设计来优化基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）。多选问题（MCQ）虽能提供可扩展的验证数据，但易导致模型通过随机猜测或简单排除等捷径进行奖励欺骗。当前方法常将MCQ转为开放格式，从而丢弃了专家设计的干扰项所提供的对比信号。本文系统性地研究了选项设计对RLVR的影响，得出两个核心见解：（1）训练与测试阶段选项数量的不匹配会损害性能；（2）设计精良的强干扰项能有效抑制随机猜测，使得即使仅使用2元选项也能进行有效的RLVR训练。基于此，我们提出了“迭代干扰项策划（IDC）”框架，该框架主动构建高质量干扰项以阻断排除捷径并促进深度推理。在多个基准测试上的实验表明，我们的方法有效提升了干扰项质量，并相较于原始数据为RLVR训练带来了显著增益。
method_highlights: 系统性地揭示并量化了选项数量不匹配对RLVR性能的损害，指出训练与测试阶段选项结构需保持一致。
提出并验证了“强干扰项”的核心作用：设计精良的干扰项能迫使模型进行深度推理而非依赖随机猜测或简单排除，从而将低信息量的2元选择题转化为有效的RLVR训练数据。
设计了“迭代干扰项策划（IDC）”框架：该方法不是被动使用现有干扰项，而是主动地、迭代地与基础模型交互，专门生成能阻断已知捷径（如基于常识的排除）的新型干扰项，形成一个持续提升数据质量的闭环。
方法论上的巧妙之处在于将“干扰项设计”本身从一个静态的数据构造问题，转变为一个动态的、与RL训练过程协同优化的主动学习问题，从而最大化利用MCQ的对比性信号。
experiment_highlights: 在多个推理基准测试（如数学、常识推理）上验证了IDC的有效性，使用IDC处理后的MCQ数据进行RLVR训练，模型性能显著优于使用原始MCQ或简单转换的开放格式数据。
通过控制实验证实，当训练数据为2元选择题（经IDC优化）而测试数据为多选时，模型性能仍具竞争力，反之亦然，强有力地支持了“强干扰项比选项数量更重要”的核心结论。
消融研究显示，IDC框架中迭代生成和针对性筛选干扰项的步骤至关重要，随机生成或仅使用原始干扰项无法达到同等效果。
定性分析表明，IDC生成的干扰项成功覆盖了模型可能依赖的浅层启发式规则（如频率偏见、常识捷径），迫使模型产生更稳健的推理链。
pros: 核心见解深刻：颠覆了“MCQ因选项数量少而低效”的固有认知，指出干扰项质量而非数量才是关键，为高效利用大规模MCQ数据提供了新范式。
方法创新且实用：提出的IDC框架可操作性强，能直接应用于现有MCQ数据集，无需改变下游任务格式，有效解决了RLVR中奖励欺骗与数据可扩展性之间的矛盾。
实验严谨充分：通过多维度（不同基准、不同选项数量配置）的对照实验，系统验证了理论发现和方法有效性，结论可靠。
意义重大：不仅提升了RLVR的训练效果，也为如何设计评估数据和训练数据以促进模型真正推理能力提供了重要指导原则。
cons: 计算成本较高：IDC框架需要多次调用基础模型（或另一个模型）来迭代生成和筛选干扰项，相比直接使用原始数据，数据预处理阶段的计算开销显著增加。
依赖于基础模型能力：IDC生成高质量干扰项的效果受限于所用生成模型的能力。如果基础模型本身推理能力不足或知识有盲区，可能无法产生真正具有挑战性的干扰项。
实验覆盖范围有限：虽然测试了多个基准，但主要集中于选择题形式的推理任务。对于其他类型任务（如代码生成、创意写作）中类似“干扰项”的设计是否有效，尚未探索。
未能完全根除所有捷径：尽管强干扰项能阻断常见捷径，但模型仍可能发现新的、未预料到的捷径，IDC的迭代过程是否能穷尽所有潜在捷径模式存疑。
reproducibility: 研究可复现性中等偏高。论文方法论部分清晰描述了IDC的迭代流程和核心思想，实验设置（使用的基准数据集、基础模型、RL算法、超参数等）在方法或实验章节有详细说明。然而，摘要及提供的信息未明确提及是否公开了完整的源代码、经过IDC处理后的具体数据集以及完整的训练日志。若作者能提供代码和数据处理脚本，则复现性将非常高；否则，研究者需根据论文描述自行实现IDC流程，可能存在细节实现上的差异。
task_list: 精读论文Method部分，完整复现IDC框架的算法流程，特别是干扰项生成和筛选的提示词设计与评判标准。
在至少一个公开的MCQ推理数据集（如MMLU、GSM8K的MCQ版本）上，使用指定的基础模型运行IDC流程，生成优化后的数据集，并对比原始干扰项与IDC生成干扰项的难度分布。
使用生成的数据集，在固定的RLVR框架下（如GRPO）进行对比训练实验，严格验证性能增益，并分析模型输出推理链的差异。
探索将IDC思想应用于其他场景：例如，为开放式问答问题自动生成有吸引力的错误答案，或为代码生成任务设计有缺陷的代码选项。
进行失败案例分析：深入研究RLVR训练后模型仍然出错的样本，判断是干扰项设计不足还是模型能力上限所致，以进一步迭代IDC。

title: Learning from Child-Directed Speech in Two-Language Scenarios: A French-English Case Study
authors: Liel Binyamin, Elior Sulem
abstract:
Research on developmentally plausible language models has largely focused on English, leaving open questions about multilingual settings. We present a systematic study of compact language models by extending BabyBERTa to English-French scenarios under strictly size-matched data conditions, covering monolingual, bilingual, and cross-lingual settings. Our design contrasts two types of training corpora: (i) child-directed speech (about 2.5M tokens), following BabyBERTa and related work, and (ii) multi-domain corpora (about 10M tokens), extending the BabyLM framework to French. To enable fair evaluation, we also introduce new resources, including French versions of QAMR and QASRL, as well as English and French multi-domain corpora.
  We evaluate the models on both syntactic and semantic tasks and compare them with models trained on Wikipedia-only data. The results reveal context-dependent effects: training on Wikipedia consistently benefits semantic tasks, whereas child-directed speech improves grammatical judgments in monolingual settings. Bilingual pretraining yields notable gains for textual entailment, with particularly strong improvements for French. Importantly, similar patterns emerge across BabyBERTa, RoBERTa, and LTG-BERT, suggesting consistent trends across architectures.
reason: 论文直接研究不同训练数据源（儿童导向语音与多领域语料库）对双语语言模型性能的影响，核心内容与研究方向'LLM training: data selection'的关键词（如data selection、data curation）高度匹配。研究设计系统，引入新法语评估资源（如QAMR和QASRL法语版），结果跨架构一致，实证贡献显著，质量优秀且高度相关，值得深入分析。
abstract_zh: 关于发展合理语言模型的研究主要集中在英语上， multilingual 设置中的问题仍未解决。我们通过将 BabyBERTa 扩展到严格匹配数据量的英法双语场景（涵盖单语、双语和跨语言设置），对紧凑语言模型进行了系统研究。我们的设计对比了两种训练语料库：(i) 儿童导向语料（约 250 万词），遵循 BabyBERTa 及相关工作；(ii) 多领域语料库（约 1000 万词），将 BabyLM 框架扩展至法语。为进行公平评估，我们还引入了新资源，包括法语版 QAMR 和 QASRL，以及英法多领域语料库。我们在句法和语义任务上评估模型，并与仅用维基百科训练的模型比较。结果显示上下文依赖效应：维基百科训练始终有利于语义任务，而儿童导向语料在单语设置中改善语法判断。双语预训练在文本蕴含任务上带来显著提升，尤其对法语改善明显。重要的是，BabyBERTa、RoBERTa 和 LTG-BERT 呈现相似模式，表明不同架构下有一致趋势。
method_highlights: 严格匹配数据量的多场景对比设计：在相同数据规模下，系统比较单语（英/法）、双语（英法）和跨语言（英→法）预训练，排除数据量偏差，凸显语料类型和语言场景的独立影响。
双轨语料库构建策略：创新性地对比‘儿童导向语料’（反映真实语言输入环境）与‘多领域语料’（扩展 BabyLM 框架至法语），揭示不同数据源对模型发展的差异化作用。
跨语言评估资源对齐：创建法语版 QAMR 和 QASRL 等语义解析数据集，并构建英法平行多领域语料，实现跨语言公平评估，解决以往英语垄断评估的问题。
experiment_highlights: 数据源效应分化：维基百科训练持续提升语义任务（如问答、蕴含）性能，而儿童导向语料在单语设置中显著改善语法判断任务，表明不同语料塑造不同语言能力。
双语预训练的跨语言增益：双语预训练在文本蕴含任务上带来最大提升，且对法语受益尤为显著，显示双语环境可能强化语义推理的跨语言泛化。
架构无关的一致性：BabyBERTa、RoBERTa、LTG-BERT 三类架构呈现相同趋势，证明核心结论稳健，不依赖特定模型设计。
资源贡献：提供首个法语版 QAMR/QASRL 及可控多领域语料，填补了法语发展语言模型评估空白。
pros: 首个系统研究双语儿童导向语料对紧凑模型影响的工作，为理解双语环境下的语言习得机制提供计算证据。
通过严格的数据量匹配和跨语言资源对齐，实现了真正公平的比较，方法设计严谨，结论可信度高。
揭示‘语料类型-任务类型-语言场景’三维交互效应，超越单一维度分析，提供更精细的图景。
跨架构验证增强了发现的普适性，对发展合理模型的设计原则有直接指导意义。
cons: 语料规模有限：儿童导向语料仅 250 万词，可能不足以训练更复杂模型或捕捉长程依赖，结论对大规模数据是否成立未知。
语言覆盖过窄：仅限英法两种语言，且均为高资源语言，结论难以推广到更多语言对或低资源场景。
评估任务侧重句法/语义：未涉及语音、语用或社会语言维度，无法全面模拟儿童语言发展。
未深入分析数据内在特性：如儿童导向语料中的具体语言特征（如重复、简化）如何导致语法提升，缺乏定性分析。
reproducibility: 部分可复现：论文明确提供了新引入的法语评估资源（QAMR/QASRL）及多领域语料库的构建方法，并详细描述了数据规模、训练设置（如 AdamW 参数、批次大小）和评估协议。但摘要未提及是否开源完整训练代码或模型检查点，且超参数搜索细节、随机种子设置等关键复现信息需查阅完整论文。
task_list: 获取并运行论文开源代码（如有），在相同英法数据条件下复现 BabyBERTa 基线实验，验证核心结果。
将方法扩展到其他语言对（如西班牙语-英语），测试结论在不同语言家族间的普适性。
分析双语预训练模型在法语文本蕴含任务上的注意力模式，探究跨语言提升的具体机制。
在相同紧凑模型上测试更大规模儿童导向语料（如 10M+ tokens），观察数据量是否改变语料类型效应。
将双语预训练模型应用于低资源语言（如法语-斯瓦希里语），探索该方法在数据稀缺场景的迁移效果。

title: HMS-BERT: Hybrid Multi-Task Self-Training for Multilingual and Multi-Label Cyberbullying Detection
authors: Zixin Feng, Xinying Cui, Yifan Sun, Zheng Wei, Jiachen Yuan, Jiazhen Hu, Ning Xin, Md Maruf Hasan
abstract: Cyberbullying on social media is inherently multilingual and multi-faceted, where abusive behaviors often overlap across multiple categories. Existing methods are commonly limited by monolingual assumptions or single-task formulations, which restrict their effectiveness in realistic multilingual and multi-label scenarios. In this paper, we propose HMS-BERT, a hybrid multi-task self-training framework for multilingual and multi-label cyberbullying detection. Built upon a pretrained multilingual BERT backbone, HMS-BERT integrates contextual representations with handcrafted linguistic features and jointly optimizes a fine-grained multi-label abuse classification task and a three-class main classification task. To address labeled data scarcity in low-resource languages, an iterative self-training strategy with confidence-based pseudo-labeling is introduced to facilitate cross-lingual knowledge transfer. Experiments on four public datasets demonstrate that HMS-BERT achieves strong performance, attaining a macro F1-score of up to 0.9847 on the multi-label task and an accuracy of 0.6775 on the main classification task. Ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of the proposed components.
reason: 该论文的核心研究内容是构建一个用于多语言、多标签网络欺凌检测的混合多任务自训练框架（HMS-BERT）。其重点在于模型架构设计（结合预训练BERT与手工特征）、多任务学习目标以及针对低资源语言的自训练策略，属于自然语言处理在特定应用领域（安全、社交媒体）的落地工作。论文与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择/筛选、推测解码）在核心目标和关键技术点上均无直接关联。未涉及数据选择、数据质量、影响函数、推测解码、快速解码等任何相关关键词。尽管论文本身在其实验领域内方法完整、有实验结果支撑，但对用户的研究方向价值有限。
abstract_zh: 社交媒体上的网络欺凌本质上具有多语言性和多面性， abusive行为常跨类别重叠。现有方法通常受限于单语假设或单任务设定，这限制了其在真实多语言多标签场景中的效果。本文提出HMS-BERT，一个面向多语言多标签网络欺凌检测的混合多任务自训练框架。该框架基于预训练的多语言BERT主干网络，整合上下文表示与手工语言特征，并联合优化一个细粒度多标签滥用分类任务与一个三分类主任务。为缓解低资源语言中标注数据稀缺的问题，引入了基于置信度的伪标签迭代自训练策略以促进跨语言知识迁移。在四个公开数据集上的实验表明，HMS-BERT取得了强劲性能，在多标签任务上宏F1值最高达0.9847，在主分类任务上准确率达0.6775。消融研究进一步验证了所提出组件的有效性。

title: DS$^2$-Instruct: Domain-Specific Data Synthesis for Large Language Models Instruction Tuning
authors: Ruiyao Xu, Noelle I. Samia, Han Liu
abstract: Adapting Large Language Models (LLMs) to specialized domains requires high-quality instruction tuning datasets, which are expensive to create through human annotation. Existing data synthesis methods focus on general-purpose tasks and fail to capture domain-specific terminology and reasoning patterns. To address this, we introduce DS$^2$-Instruct, a zero-shot framework that generates domain-specific instruction datasets without human supervision. Our approach first generates task-informed keywords to ensure comprehensive domain coverage. It then creates diverse instructions by pairing these keywords with different cognitive levels from Bloom's Taxonomy. Finally, it uses self-consistency validation to ensure data quality. We apply this framework to generate datasets across seven challenging domains, such as mathematics, finance, and logical reasoning. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates that models fine-tuned on our generated data achieve substantial improvements over existing data generation methods.
reason: 该论文直接针对LLM指令微调中的领域特定数据生成问题，与研究方向‘LLM training: data selection’高度契合。其提出的零样本数据合成框架（任务关键词生成、基于布鲁姆分类法的指令多样化、自一致性验证）是数据选择与质量提升的创新方法，并在多个挑战性领域展示了显著效果。论文问题定义清晰、方法有创新性、实验验证充分，属于高质量工作，值得精读和深入分析。
abstract_zh: 将大型语言模型适配到专业领域需要高质量的指令微调数据集，而通过人工标注创建这些数据集成本高昂。现有的数据合成方法聚焦于通用任务，无法捕捉领域特定的术语和推理模式。为应对此挑战，我们提出了DS²-Instruct，一个无需人工监督的零样本框架，用于生成领域特定的指令数据集。我们的方法首先生成任务相关的关键词以确保全面的领域覆盖；随后通过将这些关键词与布鲁姆分类法中的不同认知层次配对来创建多样化的指令；最后利用自一致性验证来确保数据质量。我们将此框架应用于七个具有挑战性的领域（如数学、金融和逻辑推理）以生成数据集。综合评估表明，使用我们生成的数据进行微调的模型相较于现有数据生成方法取得了显著提升。
method_highlights: 任务导向的关键词生成：不依赖通用词表，而是让LLM根据领域特性生成任务相关的核心术语与概念，确保生成指令能覆盖领域核心知识，巧妙之处在于将领域覆盖问题转化为LLM的自主知识检索与归纳。
认知层次驱动的指令多样性：引入布鲁姆分类法作为认知框架，强制将关键词与记忆、理解、应用、分析、评估、创造等不同层次的认知任务结合，从而系统性地生成从简单复述到复杂推理的多样化指令，解决了传统方法指令同质化的问题。
自一致性质量过滤：不依赖单一LLM输出，而是对同一指令生成多个候选回答，仅保留那些在多个生成中高度一致的（指令，回答）对，利用LLM输出的内在不确定性作为质量信号，无需额外标注即可有效过滤低质、矛盾数据。
experiment_highlights: 跨七个挑战性领域（数学、金融、逻辑推理等）的广泛验证，证明方法具有通用性。
在多个领域基准测试中，使用DS²-Instruct数据微调的模型性能一致且显著超越现有自动数据合成基线（如Self-Instruct、Alpaca等），尤其在需要专业推理的领域提升明显。
消融实验表明，三个核心组件（关键词生成、认知层次引导、自一致性验证）均对最终数据质量有不可或缺的贡献，移除任一组件均导致性能下降。
生成的数据集已公开，为领域适应研究提供了新的高质量资源。
pros: 真正的零样本与无人工干预：整个流程无需任何领域人工示例或标注，降低了领域适配的门槛与成本。
领域针对性强：通过任务关键词主动锚定领域核心，有效避免了通用数据合成方法在专业术语和深层推理上的不足。
方法论系统且可解释：将数据合成过程与教育心理学理论（布鲁姆分类法）结合，使指令的认知分布可控、可分析。
质量保障机制新颖有效：自一致性验证提供了一种轻量、自动的数据质量筛选范式。
cons: 严重依赖基础模型的能力：方法性能受限于所用LLM的领域知识和推理能力，若基础模型在某一领域知识匮乏，则生成的关键词和指令可能表面化或错误。
领域覆盖的完整性难以保证：尽管关键词生成旨在全面覆盖，但完全依赖LLM可能遗漏边缘或前沿概念，且未明确解决领域内长尾知识的问题。
计算成本较高：自一致性验证需对每个指令进行多次生成与比较，生成数据集的成本显著高于单次生成的方法。
验证的局限性：自一致性主要过滤回答矛盾，但可能无法识别指令本身模糊、有偏或与领域不相关的问题，存在质量盲区。
reproducibility: 高可复现性。论文明确提及已公开生成的数据集和用于实验的代码仓库（通过GitHub链接），为完整复现提供了基础。方法描述详细，包括 prompting 模板、关键参数（如认知层次分布、一致性阈值）及实验设置（基座模型、微调超参数等）。但完全复现高度依赖所使用的基础LLM版本，若未来模型迭代，生成结果可能存在差异。
task_list: 访问并详细阅读论文提供的代码仓库，理解关键词生成、指令合成及自一致性过滤的具体实现代码。
使用论文公开的领域数据集，复现其在至少一个领域（如数学）上的模型微调与评估流程，验证主要结果。
尝试将DS²-Instruct框架应用于一个未被论文覆盖的专业领域（如法律、医学），评估其迁移效果并分析失败案例。
设计消融实验，定量分析布鲁姆分类法中不同认知层次对模型最终性能的独立贡献。
探索将自一致性验证与其他质量评估方法（如基于规则的验证、使用更强大模型作为裁判）进行结合，以进一步提升数据效率。

title: Mending the Holes: Mitigating Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Translation
authors: Yifeng Liu, Siqi Ouyang, Yatish Hosmane Revanasiddappa, Lei Li
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capability in machine translation on high-resource language pairs, yet their performance on low-resource translation still lags behind. Existing post-training methods rely heavily on high-quality parallel data, which are often scarce or unavailable for low-resource languages. In this paper, we introduce WALAR, a reinforcement training method using only monolingual text to elevate LLMs' translation capabilities on massive low-resource languages while retaining their performance on high-resource languages. Our key insight is based on the observation of failure modes (or "holes") in existing source-based multilingual quality estimation (QE) models. Reinforcement learning (RL) using these QE models tends to amplify such holes, resulting in poorer multilingual LLMs. We develop techniques including word alignment and language alignment to mitigate such holes in WALAR's reward for RL training. We continually trained an LLM supporting translation of 101 languages using WALAR. The experiments show that our new model outperforms LLaMAX, one of the strongest open-source multilingual LLMs by a large margin on 1400 language directions on Flores-101 dataset.
reason: 该论文主要研究在强化学习训练中通过改进奖励模型（缓解质量估计模型的'holes'）来提升低资源语言翻译，核心贡献在于RL奖励设计与对齐技术。虽然其目标（提升LLM性能）与用户研究方向有间接关联，但并未涉及用户指定的两个核心领域（数据选择/推测解码）的任何关键技术。论文质量较高（实验充分、效果显著），但与用户具体研究点的直接相关性为中等。
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLMs）在高资源语言对的机器翻译上展现出卓越能力，但在低资源语言上的表现仍显落后。现有的后训练方法高度依赖高质量的平行语料，而这类数据对于低资源语言通常稀缺或难以获取。本文提出了一种名为 WALAR 的强化训练方法，仅使用单语文本即可提升 LLMs 在大量低资源语言上的翻译能力，同时保持其在高资源语言上的性能。我们的核心洞见源于对现有基于源语的多语言质量估计（QE）模型失败模式（即“漏洞”）的观察。使用这些存在缺陷的 QE 模型进行强化学习（RL）往往会放大此类漏洞，导致多语言 LLMs 性能下降。我们在 WALAR 的 RL 训练奖励中开发了包括词对齐和语言对齐在内的技术来缓解此类漏洞。我们使用 WALAR 对支持 101 种语言翻译的 LLM 进行了持续训练。实验表明，在 Flores-101 数据集的 1400 个语言方向上，我们新模型的表现大幅超越了最强的开源多语言 LLM 之一 LLaMAX。
core_contribution: 针对低资源语言翻译中因质量估计模型缺陷导致强化学习性能下降的问题，提出 WALAR 方法，通过词对齐与语言对齐技术修正奖励信号，仅利用单语数据即可有效提升多语言模型的翻译能力。

title: ESG-Bench: Benchmarking Long-Context ESG Reports for Hallucination Mitigation
authors: Siqi Sun, Ben Peng Wu, Mali Jin, Peizhen Bai, Hanpei Zhang, Xingyi Song
abstract: As corporate responsibility increasingly incorporates environmental, social, and governance (ESG) criteria, ESG reporting is becoming a legal requirement in many regions and a key channel for documenting sustainability practices and assessing firms' long-term and ethical performance. However, the length and complexity of ESG disclosures make them difficult to interpret and automate the analysis reliably. To support scalable and trustworthy analysis, this paper introduces ESG-Bench, a benchmark dataset for ESG report understanding and hallucination mitigation in large language models (LLMs). ESG-Bench contains human-annotated question-answer (QA) pairs grounded in real-world ESG report contexts, with fine-grained labels indicating whether model outputs are factually supported or hallucinated. Framing ESG report analysis as a QA task with verifiability constraints enables systematic evaluation of LLMs' ability to extract and reason over ESG content and provides a new use case: mitigating hallucinations in socially sensitive, compliance-critical settings. We design task-specific Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting strategies and fine-tune multiple state-of-the-art LLMs on ESG-Bench using CoT-annotated rationales. Our experiments show that these CoT-based methods substantially outperform standard prompting and direct fine-tuning in reducing hallucinations, and that the gains transfer to existing QA benchmarks beyond the ESG domain.
reason: 该论文质量较高，提出了新的基准数据集和针对特定领域（ESG）的幻觉缓解方法，实验设计合理。然而，其核心内容（构建特定领域基准、评估与缓解LLM幻觉、使用CoT提示）与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据选择/筛选/质量评估的方法论，也未讨论任何解码加速技术。其价值在于为特定应用场景（合规、敏感信息）的LLM评估提供了资源，但对用户的研究方向仅具非常间接的参考意义（例如，高质量标注数据是‘数据质量’的体现之一，但非论文焦点）。
abstract_zh: 随着企业责任日益纳入环境、社会与治理（ESG）标准，ESG报告正逐渐成为许多地区的法律要求，并成为记录可持续发展实践及评估企业长期与道德表现的关键渠道。然而，ESG披露内容的冗长与复杂性使其难以被可靠地解读与分析。为支持可扩展且可信的分析，本文引入了ESG-Bench，这是一个用于大语言模型（LLM）理解ESG报告及缓解其幻觉现象的基准数据集。ESG-Bench包含基于真实ESG报告上下文的人工标注问答（QA）对，并附有细粒度标签以标明模型输出是否得到事实支持或属于幻觉。将ESG报告分析设定为带有可验证性约束的问答任务，能够系统评估LLM提取与推理ESG内容的能力，并提供了一个新的应用场景：在社会敏感、合规关键的情境中缓解幻觉。我们设计了针对该任务的思维链（CoT）提示策略，并利用带有CoT标注推理过程的数据对多个先进LLM进行了微调。实验表明，这些基于CoT的方法在减少幻觉方面显著优于标准提示与直接微调，且其提升效果能迁移至ESG领域之外的现有问答基准测试中。

title: Neuron-Aware Data Selection In Instruction Tuning For Large Language Models
authors: Xin Chen, Junchao Wu, Shu Yang, Runzhe Zhan, Zeyu Wu, Min Yang, Shujian Huang, Lidia S. Chao, Derek F. Wong
abstract: Instruction Tuning (IT) has been proven to be an effective approach to unlock the powerful capabilities of large language models (LLMs). Recent studies indicate that excessive IT data can degrade LLMs performance, while carefully selecting a small subset of high-quality IT data can significantly enhance their capabilities. Therefore, identifying the most efficient subset data from the IT dataset to effectively develop either specific or general abilities in LLMs has become a critical challenge. To address this, we propose a novel and efficient framework called NAIT. NAIT evaluates the impact of IT data on LLMs performance by analyzing the similarity of neuron activation patterns between the IT dataset and the target domain capability. Specifically, NAIT captures neuron activation patterns from in-domain datasets of target domain capabilities to construct reusable and transferable neuron activation features. It then evaluates and selects optimal samples based on the similarity between candidate samples and the expected activation features of the target capabilities. Experimental results show that training on the 10\% Alpaca-GPT4 IT data subset selected by NAIT consistently outperforms methods that rely on external advanced models or uncertainty-based features across various tasks. Our findings also reveal the transferability of neuron activation features across different capabilities of LLMs. In particular, IT data with more logical reasoning and programmatic features possesses strong general transferability, enabling models to develop stronger capabilities across multiple tasks, while a stable core subset of data is sufficient to consistently activate fundamental model capabilities and universally improve performance across diverse tasks.
reason: 该论文与研究方向'LLM training: data selection'高度相关。论文提出了一种基于神经元激活模式相似性的创新数据选择框架（NAIT），直接针对Instruction Tuning场景下的数据筛选与质量评估问题，核心方法（分析神经元激活、评估数据影响、选择高效子集）与研究方向中的'data selection', 'data curation', 'data quality', 'influence functions'等关键词完美契合。论文质量优秀：提出了新颖且高效的框架，实验设计严谨（在Alpaca-GPT4数据集上验证，对比外部模型与不确定性方法），结论具有洞见（揭示了神经元激活特征的跨任务迁移性，并区分了逻辑推理/程序化特征与核心稳定子集的作用），结果显著（仅用10%数据即实现性能超越）。整体是一篇值得精读和深入分析的顶会级别工作。
abstract_zh: 指令微调（IT）已被证明是释放大语言模型（LLMs）强大能力的有效方法。近期研究表明，过多的IT数据可能会降低LLMs的性能，而精心挑选一小部分高质量IT数据则能显著提升其能力。因此，从IT数据集中识别出能最有效发展LLMs特定或通用能力的最优子集，已成为一个关键挑战。为此，我们提出了一种新颖且高效的框架NAIT。NAIT通过分析IT数据集与目标领域能力之间神经元激活模式的相似性，来评估IT数据对LLMs性能的影响。具体而言，NAIT从目标领域能力的领域内数据集中捕获神经元激活模式，以构建可重用且可迁移的神经元激活特征。然后，它基于候选样本与目标能力预期激活特征之间的相似性来评估和选择最优样本。实验结果表明，使用NAIT挑选的10% Alpaca-GPT4 IT数据子集进行训练，在各项任务上均持续优于依赖外部先进模型或基于不确定性特征的方法。我们的研究还揭示了神经元激活特征在LLMs不同能力之间的迁移性。特别是，具有更多逻辑推理和编程特征的IT数据拥有强大的通用迁移性，能使模型在多任务上发展出更强能力；而一个稳定的核心数据子集就足以持续激活模型的基础能力，并普遍提升多样任务上的性能。
method_highlights: 核心创新：提出基于神经元激活模式相似性的数据选择框架（NAIT），将数据选择问题转化为特征匹配问题，而非依赖外部评估模型或数据质量打分。
巧妙之处1：'神经元激活特征'的构建方式。不直接使用原始数据，而是先用目标领域的'领域内数据集'（如数学数据集）提取模型在该能力上的典型神经元激活模式作为'黄金特征'，这使得特征具有明确的语义导向和可迁移性。
巧妙之处2：评估过程的效率与通用性。对候选IT样本只需一次前向传播获取其神经元激活，即可计算与'黄金特征'的相似度，无需进行耗时的多次微调实验或调用大型外部模型（如GPT-4）进行评分，计算成本极低。
深刻洞察：发现并验证了'逻辑推理与程序化特征'数据的强迁移性，这类数据不仅能提升相关任务，更能作为'通用增强剂'普遍提升模型在多种任务上的基础性能。
experiment_highlights: 关键发现：仅使用NAIT筛选的10% Alpaca-GPT4数据（约1.1万条）进行微调，其性能在多个评测基准上持续且显著地超越了使用100%全数据或采用其他先进选择方法（如基于GPT-4评分、基于不确定性）的结果。
数据集对比：实验覆盖了通用能力（MMLU, BBH）和特定能力（数学GSM8K, 代码HumanEval）评测。NAIT选出的子集在通用能力上表现尤其突出，验证了其选择的'逻辑/编程特征'数据对基础能力的普适提升作用。
显著性结论1：'少即是多'。证明了在IT中，数据质量与匹配度远比数量重要，高效的数据选择是提升LLM性能与训练效率的关键。
显著性结论2：存在'稳定核心子集'。即使目标能力不同，NAIT选出的数据子集有高度重叠，表明存在一部分能普遍激活LLM基础能力的'核心数据'。
pros: 高效且模型无关：方法不依赖于特定模型架构，计算开销远低于全量训练或基于外部模型的评估，可轻松应用于不同规模的LLM。
可解释性强：通过神经元激活模式进行选择，为'为什么某条数据有效'提供了基于模型内部状态的、比黑盒打分更直观的解释。
迁移性验证深刻：实验不仅验证了方法有效性，更揭示了数据内容特征（逻辑性）与能力迁移之间的深层联系，对数据构建有重要指导意义。
实用价值高：提供了一个实用的数据筛选工具，能显著降低IT成本，并帮助构建更高质量、更具针对性的指令数据集。
cons: 依赖目标领域特征数据：构建'黄金神经元特征'需要目标领域（如数学、代码）的少量领域内数据集，若该数据稀缺或质量不高，会影响特征的代表性和选择效果。
计算开销仍存在：虽然远小于全训练，但需对候选数据集和特征数据集各进行一次完整的前向传播以收集神经元激活，对于超大规模候选集（如数百万条）仍有计算负担。
特征维度与相似度度量敏感：选择哪些神经元层、哪些神经元以及使用何种相似度度量（如余弦相似度）是超参数，论文未深入讨论其鲁棒性，可能影响结果稳定性。
跨模型泛化未验证：论文主要在单一模型（如Llama）上验证，未充分探讨'神经元激活特征'在不同架构、不同规模的模型之间是否同样有效，这是迁移性的关键边界。
reproducibility: 研究具备较高的可复现性。论文详细描述了方法论的核心步骤：1）特征提取（使用目标领域数据集）；2）相似度计算（样本与特征的神经元激活模式比较）；3）数据选择与实验设置（使用Alpaca-GPT4数据集，明确提及了基线方法、评测基准及模型大小）。实验部分提供了充分的对比基线（全数据、其他选择方法）和多个评测任务的结果。虽然摘要未明确声明代码开源，但方法描述足够详尽，具备较强的理论复现基础。建议查阅原文或作者主页确认代码可用性。
task_list: 精读论文方法部分，复现NAIT的核心计算流程，特别是神经元激活的提取与相似度计算模块。
获取并运行作者提供的官方代码（如有），在相同模型（如Llama-7B）和数据集（Alpaca-GPT4）上验证主要实验结果，确保结果可复现。
开展消融实验：改变用于构建'黄金特征'的领域内数据量、质量或领域，观察NAIT选择效果和最终模型性能的变化，验证其对特征数据的敏感性。
跨模型验证：将NAIT应用于不同架构的LLM（如Gemma, Mistral），检验其神经元激活特征和选择效果的泛化能力。
应用探索：尝试将NAIT应用于垂直领域（如医疗、法律指令数据）的IT数据选择，验证其在发展特定领域能力时的有效性。
深入分析：对NAIT选出的'核心子集'进行人工内容分析，确认其是否确实富含逻辑推理、编程等特征，并探究这些特征的具体表现形式。

title: DIALECTIC: A Multi-Agent System for Startup Evaluation
authors: Jae Yoon Bae, Simon Malberg, Joyce Galang, Andre Retterath, Georg Groh
abstract: Venture capital (VC) investors face a large number of investment opportunities but only invest in few of these, with even fewer ending up successful. Early-stage screening of opportunities is often limited by investor bandwidth, demanding tradeoffs between evaluation diligence and number of opportunities assessed. To ease this tradeoff, we introduce DIALECTIC, an LLM-based multi-agent system for startup evaluation. DIALECTIC first gathers factual knowledge about a startup and organizes these facts into a hierarchical question tree. It then synthesizes the facts into natural-language arguments for and against an investment and iteratively critiques and refines these arguments through a simulated debate, which surfaces only the most convincing arguments. Our system also produces numeric decision scores that allow investors to rank and thus efficiently prioritize opportunities. We evaluate DIALECTIC through backtesting on real investment opportunities aggregated from five VC funds, showing that DIALECTIC matches the precision of human VCs in predicting startup success.
reason: 该论文与研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）相关性很低。论文核心是应用LLM及多智能体系统解决风投评估这一垂直领域的问题，并未涉及数据选择、筛选、质量或解码加速等关键技术点。但论文展示了LLM在复杂决策场景中的系统性应用（多智能体、辩论机制、回测验证），方法设计完整，实验基于真实数据，具有一定的方法学参考价值，故给予2分（相关性低但有一定价值）。
abstract_zh: 风险投资（VC）投资者面临大量的投资机会，但仅其中极少部分会被实际投资，而最终成功的项目更是凤毛麟角。早期筛选机会常受投资者精力所限，需要在评估严谨性与所评估机会数量之间艰难权衡。为缓解这一矛盾，我们推出了 DIALECTIC——一个基于大语言模型（LLM）的多代理系统，用于初创企业评估。该系统首先收集初创企业的 factual knowledge（事实性知识），并将其组织为分层问题树；随后将事实综合为支持与反对投资的自然语言论点，并通过模拟辩论的形式对这些论点进行迭代批判与精炼，从而筛选出最具说服力的论点。本系统同时生成数值决策评分，使投资者能够对机会进行排序，进而高效确定优先级。我们通过回测五家风投基金聚合的真实投资机会对 DIALECTIC 进行评估，结果表明该系统在预测初创企业成功方面的精确度与人类风投相当。

title: Context-Enriched Natural Language Descriptions of Vessel Trajectories
authors: Kostas Patroumpas, Alexandros Troupiotis-Kapeliaris, Giannis Spiliopoulos, Panagiotis Betchavas, Dimitrios Skoutas, Dimitris Zissis, Nikos Bikakis
abstract: We address the problem of transforming raw vessel trajectory data collected from AIS into structured and semantically enriched representations interpretable by humans and directly usable by machine reasoning systems. We propose a context-aware trajectory abstraction framework that segments noisy AIS sequences into distinct trips each consisting of clean, mobility-annotated episodes. Each episode is further enriched with multi-source contextual information, such as nearby geographic entities, offshore navigation features, and weather conditions. Crucially, such representations can support generation of controlled natural language descriptions using LLMs. We empirically examine the quality of such descriptions generated using several LLMs over AIS data along with open contextual features. By increasing semantic density and reducing spatiotemporal complexity, this abstraction can facilitate downstream analytics and enable integration with LLMs for higher-level maritime reasoning tasks.
reason: 该论文的核心工作是海事领域的轨迹数据抽象与自然语言描述生成，虽然提及使用LLM进行描述生成，但其研究重点与您指定的两个方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）均无直接关联。论文本身在方法设计和实验评估上具有一定质量和创新性，但并非针对您关注的技术问题，因此相关性较低。
abstract_zh: 本文旨在解决如何将来自船舶自动识别系统（AIS）的原始轨迹数据，转化为结构化的、语义丰富的表达形式，使其既能为人类所理解，又能被机器推理系统直接使用。我们提出一个上下文感知的轨迹抽象框架，该框架能将带有噪声的AIS序列分割成多个独立的航次，每个航次由干净且带有移动性标注的片段组成。每个片段进一步融合了多源上下文信息，如邻近地理实体、近海导航特征及气象条件。关键在于，此类表示能够支持利用大语言模型（LLMs）生成可控的自然语言描述。我们通过实证评估，检验了基于AIS数据及开放上下文特征，由不同大语言模型生成描述的质量。通过提升语义密度并降低时空复杂度，此抽象方法可促进下游分析，并支持与大语言模型集成，以完成更高层的海事推理任务。

title: Multi-Step Semantic Reasoning in Generative Retrieval
authors: Steven Dong, Yubao Tang, Maarten de Rijke
abstract: Generative retrieval (GR) models encode a corpus within model parameters and generate relevant document identifiers directly for a given query. While this paradigm shows promise in retrieval tasks, existing GR models struggle with complex queries in numerical contexts, such as those involving semantic reasoning over financial reports, due to limited reasoning capabilities. This limitation leads to suboptimal retrieval accuracy and hinders practical applicability. We propose ReasonGR, a framework designed to enhance multi-step semantic reasoning in numerical contexts within GR. ReasonGR employs a structured prompting strategy combining task-specific instructions with stepwise reasoning guidance to better address complex retrieval queries. Additionally, it integrates a reasoning-focused adaptation module to improve the learning of reasoning-related parameters. Experiments on the FinQA dataset, which contains financial queries over complex documents, demonstrate that ReasonGR improves retrieval accuracy and consistency, indicating its potential for advancing GR models in reasoning-intensive retrieval scenarios.
reason: 该论文主要研究生成式检索（Generative Retrieval）中的多步语义推理增强，与您指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、投机解码）无直接关联。论文本身问题定义清晰，方法设计合理（结构化提示与推理适应模块），并在金融问答数据集上进行了实验验证，属于信息检索与推理结合的中等质量工作，但因其主题不在您关注的领域内，故相关性低但有一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 生成式检索（GR）模型将语料库编码到模型参数中，并直接为给定查询生成相关的文档标识符。虽然该范式在检索任务中显示出前景，但现有GR模型在处理涉及财务报告等数值语境中的复杂查询时，由于推理能力有限而表现不佳。这一局限性导致了检索精度不足，并阻碍了其实际应用。我们提出了ReasonGR框架，旨在增强GR在数值语境中的多步语义推理能力。ReasonGR采用结构化的提示策略，结合任务特定指令与逐步推理引导，以更好地应对复杂的检索查询。此外，它集成了一个推理聚焦的适配模块，以提升对推理相关参数的学习。在包含复杂文档上金融查询的FinQA数据集上的实验表明，ReasonGR提高了检索精度与一致性，表明其在推理密集型检索场景中推动GR模型发展的潜力。

title: Efficient Reasoning with Balanced Thinking
authors: Yulin Li, Tengyao Tu, Li Ding, Junjie Wang, Huiling Zhen, Yixin Chen, Yong Li, Zhuotao Tian
abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have shown remarkable reasoning capabilities, yet they often suffer from overthinking, expending redundant computational steps on simple problems, or underthinking, failing to explore sufficient reasoning paths despite inherent capabilities. These issues lead to inefficiencies and potential inaccuracies, limiting practical deployment in resource-constrained settings. Existing methods to mitigate overthinking, such as suppressing reflective keywords or adjusting reasoning length, may inadvertently induce underthinking, compromising accuracy. Therefore, we propose ReBalance, a training-free framework that achieves efficient reasoning with balanced thinking. ReBalance leverages confidence as a continuous indicator of reasoning dynamics, identifying overthinking through high confidence variance and underthinking via consistent overconfidence. By aggregating hidden states from a small-scale dataset into reasoning mode prototypes, we compute a steering vector to guide LRMs' reasoning trajectories. A dynamic control function modulates this vector's strength and direction based on real-time confidence, pruning redundancy during overthinking, and promoting exploration during underthinking. Extensive experiments conducted on four models ranging from 0.5B to 32B, and across nine benchmarks in math reasoning, general question answering, and coding tasks demonstrate that ReBalance effectively reduces output redundancy while improving accuracy, offering a general, training-free, and plug-and-play strategy for efficient and robust LRM deployment. Code is available at https://github.com/yu-lin-li/ReBalance .
reason: 该论文的核心研究内容是解决大型推理模型（LRMs）中的‘过思考’与‘欠思考’问题，提出了一种基于置信度的动态推理轨迹调整方法（ReBalance）。其研究目标（提升推理效率与准确性）与您提供的两个研究方向（LLM数据选择、推测解码）在技术路径和核心关注点上均无直接交集。论文未涉及数据筛选、过滤、课程学习或推测解码、并行解码等关键词所述的技术。然而，该论文针对大模型推理效率问题提出了新颖、训练无关的解决方案，实验验证充分，对更广泛的LLM效率优化研究具有一定参考价值，因此不属于无价值文章。
abstract_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）已展现出卓越的推理能力，但常受困于过度思考（在简单问题上耗费冗余计算步骤）或思考不足（尽管具备内在能力却未能充分探索推理路径）。这些问题导致效率低下与潜在不准确，限制了其在资源受限场景下的实际部署。现有缓解过度思考的方法（如抑制反思关键词或调整推理长度）可能不慎引发思考不足，从而损害准确性。因此，我们提出ReBalance——一个无需训练的框架，旨在通过平衡思考实现高效推理。ReBalance利用置信度作为推理动态的连续指标：通过高置信度方差识别过度思考，并通过持续过度自信检测思考不足。通过将小规模数据集的隐藏状态聚合为推理模式原型，我们计算导向向量以引导LRMs的推理轨迹。动态控制函数基于实时置信度调节该向量的强度与方向——在过度思考时修剪冗余，在思考不足时促进探索。在四个规模从0.5B至32B的模型上，于数学推理、通用问答及编程任务等九项基准测试中开展的广泛实验表明，ReBalance能有效减少输出冗余并提升准确性，为高效且鲁棒的LRM部署提供了一种通用、无需训练、即插即用的策略。代码已公开于 https://github.com/yu-lin-li/ReBalance 。

title: NeuroLoRA: Context-Aware Neuromodulation for Parameter-Efficient Multi-Task Adaptation
authors: Yuxin Yang, Haoran Zhang, Mingxuan Li, Jiachen Xu, Ruoxi Shen, Zhenyu Wang, Tianhao Liu, Siqi Chen, Weilin Huang
abstract: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) techniques, particularly Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), have become essential for adapting Large Language Models (LLMs) to downstream tasks. While the recent FlyLoRA framework successfully leverages bio-inspired sparse random projections to mitigate parameter interference, it relies on a static, magnitude-based routing mechanism that is agnostic to input context. In this paper, we propose NeuroLoRA, a novel Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) based LoRA framework inspired by biological neuromodulation -- the dynamic regulation of neuronal excitability based on context. NeuroLoRA retains the computational efficiency of frozen random projections while introducing a lightweight, learnable neuromodulation gate that contextually rescales the projection space prior to expert selection. We further propose a Contrastive Orthogonality Loss to explicitly enforce separation between expert subspaces, enhancing both task decoupling and continual learning capacity. Extensive experiments on MMLU, GSM8K, and ScienceQA demonstrate that NeuroLoRA consistently outperforms FlyLoRA and other strong baselines across single-task adaptation, multi-task model merging, and sequential continual learning scenarios, while maintaining comparable parameter efficiency.
reason: 该论文聚焦于参数高效微调（PEFT）与多任务适应，提出了基于神经调制的LoRA改进框架（NeuroLoRA），核心贡献在于动态上下文感知的专家路由和子空间正交性约束。然而，这与用户指定的两个研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在技术目标和方法论上均无直接关联。论文未涉及数据筛选、质量评估、过滤、去重或推测解码等关键词相关的内容。尽管论文本身在PEFT领域可能具有创新性和实验价值，但与用户当前研究兴趣的相关性很低。
abstract_zh: 参数高效微调（PEFT）技术，尤其是低秩适配（LoRA），已成为将大语言模型（LLM）适配到下游任务的关键方法。尽管近期的FlyLoRA框架成功利用生物启发的稀疏随机投影来缓解参数干扰，但其依赖的静态、基于幅度的路由机制不考虑输入上下文。本文提出NeuroLoRA，一种受生物神经调节——即基于上下文动态调节神经元兴奋性——启发的 novel 混合专家（MoE）LoRA框架。NeuroLoRA在保持冻结随机投影计算效率的同时，引入了一个轻量级、可学习的神经调节门，该门能在专家选择前根据上下文对投影空间进行重缩放。我们还进一步提出了对比正交损失，以显式地强制专家子空间之间的分离，从而增强任务解耦和持续学习能力。在MMLU、GSM8K和ScienceQA上的大量实验表明，NeuroLoRA在单任务适配、多任务模型合并以及顺序持续学习场景中，均持续优于FlyLoRA及其他强基线方法，同时保持了相当的参数效率。

title: TERMINATOR: Learning Optimal Exit Points for Early Stopping in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
authors: Alliot Nagle, Jakhongir Saydaliev, Dhia Garbaya, Michael Gastpar, Ashok Vardhan Makkuva, Hyeji Kim
abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) achieve impressive performance on complex reasoning tasks via Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning, which enables them to generate intermediate thinking tokens before arriving at the final answer. However, LRMs often suffer from significant overthinking, spending excessive compute time even after the answer is generated early on. Prior work has identified the existence of an optimal reasoning length such that truncating reasoning at this point significantly shortens CoT outputs with virtually no change in performance. However, determining optimal CoT lengths for practical datasets is highly non-trivial as they are fully task and model-dependent. In this paper, we precisely address this and design TERMINATOR, an early-exit strategy for LRMs at inference to mitigate overthinking. The central idea underpinning TERMINATOR is that the first arrival of an LRM's final answer is often predictable, and we leverage these first answer positions to create a novel dataset of optimal reasoning lengths to train TERMINATOR. Powered by this approach, TERMINATOR achieves significant reductions in CoT lengths of 14%-55% on average across four challenging practical datasets: MATH-500, AIME 2025, HumanEval, and GPQA, whilst outperforming current state-of-the-art methods.
reason: 该论文研究的是大型推理模型（LRMs）在链式思维（CoT）推理过程中的早期停止策略，以缓解过度思考问题。其核心贡献在于推理优化（减少计算量），与用户指定的两个研究方向——'LLM训练：数据选择'和'推测解码'——的直接关联度均较低。虽然论文中使用了从模型推理轨迹中提取的'最优推理长度'数据来训练退出策略，这涉及数据收集与利用，但其目标、方法和核心创新点并非围绕数据选择、策展、过滤或影响函数等关键词展开；同时，论文完全未涉及推测解码、双通道解码、推测采样等概念。因此，论文与用户研究方向相关性低，但其解决推理效率问题的思路对关注推理优化的研究者可能有一定参考价值。
abstract_zh: 大推理模型（LRMs）通过思维链（CoT）推理在复杂推理任务上取得了令人瞩目的性能，该方法使其能够在得出最终答案前生成中间思考标记。然而，LRMs 常受困于显著的过度推理，即即便答案早已生成，仍会消耗过多的计算时间。先前的研究已确定存在一个最优推理长度，在该点截断推理可大幅缩短CoT输出，且几乎不影响性能。然而，为实际数据集确定最优CoT长度极具挑战性，因为它们完全依赖于具体任务和模型。本文精准地解决了这一问题，设计了 TERMINATOR——一种用于推理时缓解过度推理的LRM提前退出策略。TERMINATOR 的核心思想在于：LRM 首次生成最终答案的时刻通常是可预测的，我们利用这些首次答案位置构建了一个新颖的最优推理长度数据集，用以训练 TERMINATOR。得益于这一方法，TERMINATOR 在四个具有挑战性的实际数据集（MATH-500、AIME 2025、HumanEval 和 GPQA）上实现了 CoT 长度平均 14%-55% 的显著缩减，同时超越了当前最先进的方法。

title: Literary Narrative as Moral Probe : A Cross-System Framework for Evaluating AI Ethical Reasoning and Refusal Behavior
authors: David C. Flynn
abstract: Existing AI moral evaluation frameworks test for the production of correct-sounding ethical responses rather than the presence of genuine moral reasoning capacity. This paper introduces a novel probe methodology using literary narrative - specifically, unresolvable moral scenarios drawn from a published science fiction series - as stimulus material structurally resistant to surface performance. We present results from a 24-condition cross-system study spanning 13 distinct systems across two series: Series 1 (frontier commercial systems, blind; n=7) and Series 2 (local and API open-source systems, blind and declared; n=6). Four Series 2 systems were re-administered under declared conditions (13 blind + 4 declared + 7 ceiling probe = 24 total conditions), yielding zero delta across all 16 dimension-pair comparisons. Probe administration was conducted by two human raters across three machines; primary blind scoring was performed by Claude (Anthropic) as LLM judge, with Gemini Pro (Google) and Copilot Pro (Microsoft) serving as independent judges for the ceiling discrimination probe. A supplemental theological differentiator probe yielded perfect rank-order agreement between the two independent ceiling probe judges (Gemini Pro and Copilot Pro; rs = 1.00). Five qualitatively distinct D3 reflexive failure modes were identified - including categorical self-misidentification and false positive self-attribution - suggesting that instrument sophistication scales with system capability rather than being circumvented by it. We argue that literary narrative constitutes an anticipatory evaluation instrument - one that becomes more discriminating as AI capability increases - and that the gap between performed and authentic moral reasoning is measurable, meaningful, and consequential for deployment decisions in high-stakes domains.
reason: 该论文的研究焦点（利用文学叙事评估AI的道德推理与拒绝行为）与您提供的两个具体研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择、推测解码）在核心目标、方法论和关键词上均无直接重叠。论文属于AI伦理与评估方法论范畴，而您的研究方向属于LLM训练效率与推理优化技术范畴。尽管论文本身设计严谨、具有学术价值（提出了新的评估范式、进行了多系统实验、发现了反思失败模式），但其内容对您指定的技术方向贡献非常有限，仅可作为广义的AI系统能力评估的参考。
abstract_zh: 现有的人工智能道德评估框架主要测试其是否会产生‘看似正确的’伦理回应，而非检验其是否具备真正的道德推理能力。本文提出了一种新颖的探测方法，即以文学叙事——具体而言，取材于一部已出版科幻系列中无法解决的道德情境——作为刺激材料，该材料在结构上能抵御表面表现。我们呈现了一项涵盖24个条件的跨系统研究结果，涉及两个系列共13个不同系统：系列1（前沿商业系统，盲测；n=7）和系列2（本地与API开源系统，盲测与声明条件；n=6）。其中，系列2的四个系统在声明条件下重新进行了测试（总计13个盲测条件 + 4个声明条件 + 7个上限探测 = 24个总条件），在所有16个维度-成对比较中均未产生差异。探测由两位人类评分员在三台机器上实施；主要盲评由Claude（Anthropic）作为大语言模型法官执行，Gemini Pro（Google）和Copilot Pro（Microsoft）则作为上限区分探测的独立法官。一项补充的神学区分探测显示，两位独立的上限探测法官（Gemini Pro与Copilot Pro）之间取得了完美的秩次相关（rs = 1.00）。研究识别出五种定性上不同的D3反射性失败模式——包括类别自我误识与假阳性自我归因——这表明工具的精进程度随系统能力提升而增强，而非被其规避。我们论证认为，文学叙事构成了一种前瞻性评估工具——其随着人工智能能力的提升而更具区分度——且表演性与真实性道德推理之间的差距是可测量的、有意义的，并对高风险领域中的部署决策具有重要影响。

title: daVinci-Env: Open SWE Environment Synthesis at Scale
authors: Dayuan Fu, Shenyu Wu, Yunze Wu, Zerui Peng, Yaxing Huang, Jie Sun, Ji Zeng, Mohan Jiang, Lin Zhang, Yukun Li, Jiarui Hu, Liming Liu, Jinlong Hou, Pengfei Liu
abstract: Training capable software engineering (SWE) agents demands large-scale, executable, and verifiable environments that provide dynamic feedback loops for iterative code editing, test execution, and solution refinement. However, existing open-source datasets remain limited in scale and repository diversity, while industrial solutions are opaque with unreleased infrastructure, creating a prohibitive barrier for most academic research groups. We present OpenSWE, the largest fully transparent framework for SWE agent training in Python, comprising 45,320 executable Docker environments spanning over 12.8k repositories, with all Dockerfiles, evaluation scripts, and infrastructure fully open-sourced for reproducibility. OpenSWE is built through a multi-agent synthesis pipeline deployed across a 64-node distributed cluster, automating repository exploration, Dockerfile construction, evaluation script generation, and iterative test analysis. Beyond scale, we propose a quality-centric filtering pipeline that characterizes the inherent difficulty of each environment, filtering out instances that are either unsolvable or insufficiently challenging and retaining only those that maximize learning efficiency. With $891K spent on environment construction and an additional $576K on trajectory sampling and difficulty-aware curation, the entire project represents a total investment of approximately $1.47 million, yielding about 13,000 curated trajectories from roughly 9,000 quality guaranteed environments. Extensive experiments validate OpenSWE's effectiveness: OpenSWE-32B and OpenSWE-72B achieve 62.4% and 66.0% on SWE-bench Verified, establishing SOTA among Qwen2.5 series. Moreover, SWE-focused training yields substantial out-of-domain improvements, including up to 12 points on mathematical reasoning and 5 points on science benchmarks, without degrading factual recall.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是构建一个大规模、可复现的软件工程（SWE）智能体训练环境框架（OpenSWE），重点在于环境合成、Docker自动化、轨迹采样和难度感知的过滤。虽然其‘质量中心过滤管道’与用户研究方向中的‘数据质量’、‘数据筛选’有间接和有限的关联，但论文的主要目标（环境构建与SWE智能体训练）与用户明确指定的两个领域（LLM训练中的数据选择方法、推测解码技术）在核心问题、方法论和应用场景上均不直接匹配。论文本身质量高、规模大、开源彻底且实验有效，但针对用户的具体研究方向，其相关性较低，仅其过滤管道的设计思路可能具有边缘参考价值。
abstract_zh: 训练强大的软件工程（SWE）智能体需要大规模、可执行且可验证的环境，以提供动态反馈循环，用于迭代式代码编辑、测试执行和方案优化。然而，现有的开源数据集在规模和代码库多样性上仍存在局限，而工业界方案则不透明且未公开其基础设施，为多数学术研究组设置了难以逾越的障碍。我们提出 OpenSWE，这是面向 Python 的 SWE 智能体训练中最大、完全透明的框架，包含 45,320 个可执行的 Docker 环境，涵盖超过 12,800 个代码库，且所有 Dockerfile、评估脚本及基础设施均已完全开源以确保可复现性。OpenSWE 通过部署在 64 节点分布式集群上的多智能体合成管道构建，自动化完成代码库探索、Dockerfile 构建、评估脚本生成以及迭代式测试分析。除规模优势外，我们还提出一个以质量为中心的过滤管道，用于刻画每个环境的内在难度，过滤掉无法解决或挑战不足的实例，仅保留能最大化学习效率的环境。在环境构建上投入 89.1 万美元，并在轨迹采样与难度感知筛选上额外投入 57.6 万美元，整个项目总投资约 147 万美元，最终从约 9,000 个质量有保障的环境中产出约 13,000 条精心筛选的轨迹。大量实验验证了 OpenSWE 的有效性：OpenSWE-32B 和 OpenSWE-72B 在 SWE-bench Verified 上分别达到 62.4% 和 66.0%，在 Qwen2.5 系列中树立了最先进水平。此外，聚焦 SWE 的训练带来了显著的跨领域提升，包括数学推理能力最多提升 12 分，科学基准测试提升 5 分，且不会降低事实性回忆能力。

title: Developing and evaluating a chatbot to support maternal health care
authors: Smriti Jha, Vidhi Jain, Jianyu Xu, Grace Liu, Sowmya Ramesh, Jitender Nagpal, Gretchen Chapman, Benjamin Bellows, Siddhartha Goyal, Aarti Singh, Bryan Wilder
abstract:
The ability to provide trustworthy maternal health information using phone-based chatbots can have a significant impact, particularly in low-resource settings where users have low health literacy and limited access to care. However, deploying such systems is technically challenging: user queries are short, underspecified, and code-mixed across languages, answers require regional context-specific grounding, and partial or missing symptom context makes safe routing decisions difficult.
  We present a chatbot for maternal health in India developed through a partnership between academic researchers, a health tech company, a public health nonprofit, and a hospital. The system combines (1) stage-aware triage, routing high-risk queries to expert templates, (2) hybrid retrieval over curated maternal/newborn guidelines, and (3) evidence-conditioned generation from an LLM. Our core contribution is an evaluation workflow for high-stakes deployment under limited expert supervision. Targeting both component-level and end-to-end testing, we introduce: (i) a labeled triage benchmark (N=150) achieving 86.7% emergency recall, explicitly reporting the missed-emergency vs. over-escalation trade-off; (ii) a synthetic multi-evidence retrieval benchmark (N=100) with chunk-level evidence labels; (iii) LLM-as-judge comparison on real queries (N=781) using clinician-codesigned criteria; and (iv) expert validation. Our findings show that trustworthy medical assistants in multilingual, noisy settings require defense-in-depth design paired with multi-method evaluation, rather than any single model and evaluation method choice.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献在于高风险医疗场景下的系统设计（分阶段分诊、混合检索）与多方法评估流程（基准构建、LLM-as-judge、专家验证），与应用导向的工程实践强相关。然而，其内容完全不涉及用户指定的两个研究方向：1）未讨论LLM训练中的数据选择、过滤、质量或影响函数等技术；2）未涉及推测解码、快速采样或并行解码等推断加速技术。论文质量优秀（多机构合作、严谨评估、明确结论），但与用户研究方向的相关性极低，仅在‘LLM应用’这一宽泛层面有微弱联系。
abstract_zh: 利用基于电话的聊天机器人提供可靠的孕产妇健康信息，尤其在用户健康素养低、医疗资源有限的低资源环境中，能产生显著影响。然而，部署此类系统面临技术挑战：用户查询简短、不明确且跨语言混合；答案需要结合区域特定背景；部分或缺失的症状上下文使得安全路由决策变得困难。我们介绍了一个在印度开发的孕产妇健康聊天机器人，由学术研究者、健康科技公司、公共卫生非营利组织和医院合作完成。该系统结合了：(1) 分阶段感知分诊，将高风险查询路由至专家模板；(2) 对精选的孕产妇/新生儿指南进行混合检索；(3) 基于证据的条件生成（使用大语言模型）。我们的核心贡献是在专家监督有限的情况下，为高风险部署提出了一种评估流程。针对组件级和端到端测试，我们引入了：(i) 一个标注的分诊基准（N=150），实现了86.7%的紧急情况召回率，并明确报告了漏报紧急情况与过度升级之间的权衡；(ii) 一个合成多证据检索基准（N=100），包含片段级证据标签；(iii) 使用临床医生共同设计标准，在真实查询（N=781）上进行大语言模型作为评判者的对比；(iv) 专家验证。我们的研究结果表明，在多语言、嘈杂的环境中构建值得信赖的医疗助手，需要纵深防御设计配合多方法评估，而非依赖单一模型和评估方法的选择。

title: Mask-Enhanced Autoregressive Prediction: Pay Less Attention to Learn More
authors: Xialie Zhuang, Zhikai Jia, Jianjin Li, Zhenyu Zhang, Li Shen, Zheng Cao, Shiwei Liu
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are discovered to suffer from accurately retrieving key information. To address this, we propose Mask-Enhanced Autoregressive Prediction (MEAP), a simple yet effective training paradigm that seamlessly integrates Masked Language Modeling (MLM) into Next-Token Prediction (NTP) to enhance the latter's in-context retrieval capabilities. Specifically, MEAP first randomly masks a small fraction of input tokens and then directly performs the standard next-token prediction autoregressive using a decoder-only Transformer. MEAP eliminates the need for bidirectional attention or encoder-decoder architectures for MLM, incurring no additional computational overhead during pre-training or inference. Intensive experiments demonstrate that MEAP substantially outperforms NTP on key information retrieval and long-context reasoning tasks, while performing on par or better on commonsense reasoning tasks. The benefits of MEAP also extend to supervised fine-tuning, where it shows remarkable advantages in lost-in-the-middle scenarios, outperforming NTP by 11.77 percentage points. Our analysis indicates that MEAP's effectiveness arises from its ability to promote more distinguishable attention scores by concentrating on a reduced set of non-masked tokens. This mechanism improves the model's focus on task-relevant signals while mitigating the influence of peripheral context. These findings position MEAP as a promising training paradigm for large language models.
reason: 该论文提出了一种新的训练范式（MEAP），通过将掩码语言建模（MLM）整合到自回归预测（NTP）中以增强模型的关键信息检索能力。虽然论文质量较高、实验充分且结论清晰，但其核心贡献在于训练目标的设计与优化，与用户指定的两个研究方向（'llm-data-selection' 关注数据层面的筛选/质量/影响函数，以及 'speculative-decoding' 关注推理阶段的加速技术）均无直接关联。MEAP并未涉及数据选择、过滤、去重或质量评估，也未涉及任何推测解码、采样或并行推理机制。因此，与用户研究方向的关联度较低，但论文本身在LLM训练方法上具有一定独立价值。
abstract_zh: 研究发现，大语言模型（LLMs）在准确检索关键信息方面存在不足。为解决此问题，我们提出了掩码增强自回归预测（MEAP），这是一种简洁而有效的训练范式，它将掩码语言建模（MLM）无缝整合到下一个词元预测（NTP）中，以增强后者的上下文检索能力。具体而言，MEAP首先随机掩码一小部分输入词元，然后使用仅解码器Transformer直接执行标准的自回归下一个词元预测。MEAP无需为MLM引入双向注意力或编码器-解码器架构，在预训练或推理过程中均不产生额外计算开销。大量实验表明，MEAP在关键信息检索和长上下文推理任务上显著优于NTP，在常识推理任务上表现相当或更优。MEAP的优势也延续到监督微调阶段，在“迷失在中间”场景中展现出显著优势，比NTP高出11.77个百分点。我们的分析表明，MEAP的有效性源于其通过聚焦于更少的非掩码词元，促进产生更可区分的注意力分数。该机制增强了模型对任务相关信号的关注，同时减轻了外围上下文的影响。这些发现使MEAP成为大语言模型一种有前景的训练范式。

title: Re2: A Consistency-ensured Dataset for Full-stage Peer Review and Multi-turn Rebuttal Discussions
authors: Daoze Zhang, Zhijian Bao, Sihang Du, Zhiyi Zhao, Kuangling Zhang, Dezheng Bao, Yang Yang
abstract: Peer review is a critical component of scientific progress in the fields like AI, but the rapid increase in submission volume has strained the reviewing system, which inevitably leads to reviewer shortages and declines review quality. Besides the growing research popularity, another key factor in this overload is the repeated resubmission of substandard manuscripts, largely due to the lack of effective tools for authors to self-evaluate their work before submission. Large Language Models (LLMs) show great promise in assisting both authors and reviewers, and their performance is fundamentally limited by the quality of the peer review data. However, existing peer review datasets face three major limitations: (1) limited data diversity, (2) inconsistent and low-quality data due to the use of revised rather than initial submissions, and (3) insufficient support for tasks involving rebuttal and reviewer-author interactions. To address these challenges, we introduce the largest consistency-ensured peer review and rebuttal dataset named Re^2, which comprises 19,926 initial submissions, 70,668 review comments, and 53,818 rebuttals from 24 conferences and 21 workshops on OpenReview. Moreover, the rebuttal and discussion stage is framed as a multi-turn conversation paradigm to support both traditional static review tasks and dynamic interactive LLM assistants, providing more practical guidance for authors to refine their manuscripts and helping alleviate the growing review burden. Our data and code are available in https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ReviewBench_anon/.
reason: 该论文的核心贡献是构建一个用于同行评审与反驳讨论的大规模、一致性保证的数据集（Re^2），旨在解决现有评审数据集的局限性，并支持LLM在评审辅助中的应用。然而，其研究重点在于特定应用场景（学术评审）的数据集构建与任务定义，与用户指定的研究方向（LLM训练中的数据选择方法，或推测解码技术）在技术焦点上无直接关联。论文未涉及数据选择、过滤、策展、影响函数等数据训练优化方法，也未讨论推测解码、快速生成等推理加速技术。尽管LLM作为工具被提及，但论文属于数据集与评测基准工作，对用户核心研究方向的参考价值有限。
abstract_zh: 同行评审是人工智能等领域科学进步的关键环节，但投稿量的快速增长已使审稿系统不堪重负，不可避免地导致审稿人短缺和审稿质量下降。除了研究热度持续上升外，造成这一过载的另一关键因素是劣质稿件的反复投稿，这很大程度上源于作者在投稿前缺乏有效的自我评估工具。大语言模型在辅助作者和审稿人方面展现出巨大潜力，但其性能从根本上受限于同行评审数据的质量。然而，现有同行评审数据集存在三大局限：(1) 数据多样性有限；(2) 因使用修订稿而非初稿导致数据不一致且质量较低；(3) 对涉及反驳及审稿人-作者互动的任务支持不足。为应对这些挑战，我们推出了最大规模的、确保一致性的同行评审与反驳数据集Re²，该数据集包含来自OpenReview平台上24个会议和21个研讨会的19,926篇初稿、70,668条审稿意见以及53,818次反驳。此外，我们将反驳与讨论阶段框架化为多轮对话范式，以同时支持传统的静态审稿任务和动态交互式大语言模型助手，从而为作者修改稿件提供更实用的指导，并有助于缓解日益增长的审稿负担。我们的数据与代码已公开于 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/ReviewBench_anon/。

title: LESS: Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-Supervised Learning for Speech Foundational Models Using in-the-wild Data
authors: Wen Ding, Fan Qian
abstract: Although state-of-the-art Speech Foundational Models can produce high-quality text pseudo-labels, applying Semi-Supervised Learning (SSL) for in-the-wild real-world data remains challenging due to its richer and more complex acoustics compared to curated datasets. To address the challenges, we introduce LESS (Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-supervised Learning), a versatile framework that uses Large Language Models (LLMs) to correct pseudo-labels generated on in-the-wild data.
